# Topic Dossier: autoplots

Accessible conversation excerpts tagged by keyword. Source dump, not polished synthesis.


## 1. 2026-07-03T16:50:51.040Z - autoquant_origin - user

That’s a great name for the Codex sandbox.

I’d use Analytics Shiny App as the place to build the product shell around AutoPlots without risking AutoPlots itself.

The project mission should be:

Analytics Shiny App is a local-first Shiny/Electron visualization builder powered by AutoPlots.

It lets users:
- load datasets
- create AutoPlots charts
- arrange plots into grids/tabs/sections
- preview outputs
- export self-contained HTML
- export PNG
- export reproducible R code

The first AGENTS.md / Codex instruction file should be strict:

# Analytics Shiny App Doctrine

This app is a visual builder around AutoPlots.

Do not redesign AutoPlots.
Do not change AutoPlots public APIs.
Do not introduce nested plot configuration objects.
Do not replace AutoPlots calls with ggplot2, plotly, echarts4r verbs, or custom JS chart builders.

Generated plot code must use AutoPlots high-level functions like:

AutoPlots::Area(
  dt = data,
  XVar = "DateTime",
  YVar = "Revenue",
  Theme = "dark",
  title.text = "Revenue Trend"
)

Generated layout code must use:

AutoPlots::display_plots_grid()
AutoPlots::display_plots_tabs()
AutoPlots::display_plots_sections()

The app should remain local-first.
The app should export:
- self-contained HTML
- PNG
- reproducible R code

Prefer small, reviewable changes.

For the first Codex task, I’d keep it extremely bounded:

Create the initial Shiny app skeleton.

Requirements:
- app.R at project root
- app loads AutoPlots
- app has tabs: Data, Plots, Layout, Export
- Data tab can upload one CSV and preview it
- Plots tab lets user choose PlotType from Area, Line, Bar, Scatter, Histogram
- User can choose XVar, YVar, GroupVar from uploaded data columns
- User can enter title.text
- App renders one preview plot
- App shows generated AutoPlots R code
- Do not implement multi-plot layout yet
- Do not implement Electron packaging yet

Then second task:

Add multi-plot support.

Requirements:
- User can add current preview plot to a plot list
- Show list of created plots with names p1, p2, p3
- Layout tab previews AutoPlots::display_plots_sections()
- Generated code includes plot creation code and display_plots_sections() code

That progression keeps it safe and gets you to the real product quickly.

library(shiny)
library(AutoPlots)

plot_types <- c("Area", "Line", "Bar", "Scatter", "Histogram")

empty_choice <- function(label = "(none)") {
  stats::setNames("", label)
}

column_choices <- function(data, include_none = FALSE) {
  if (is.null(data)) {
    return(empty_choice())
  }

  choices <- names(data)
  if (include_none) {
    return(c(empty_choice(), choices))
  }

  choices
}

r_string <- function(value) {
  if (is.null(value) || identical(value, "")) {
    return("NULL")
  }

  deparse(value, width.cutoff = 500L)
}

selected_value <- function(value) {
  if (is.null(value) || identical(value, "")) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  value
}

build_autoplots_call <- function(plot_type, data, x_var, y_var, group_var, title_text) {
  plot_fun <- getExportedValue("AutoPlots", plot_type)
  args <- list(
    dt = data,
    XVar = selected_value(x_var),
    YVar = selected_value(y_var),
    GroupVar = selected_value(group_var),
    title.text = selected_value(title_text)
  )

  args <- args[!vapply(args, is.null, logical(1))]
  do.call(plot_fun, args)
}

build_autoplots_code <- function(plot_type, x_var, y_var, group_var, title_text) {
  args <- c(
    "dt = data",
    paste0("XVar = ", r_string(selected_value(x_var))),
    paste0("YVar = ", r_string(selected_value(y_var))),
    if (!is.null(selected_value(group_var))) {
      paste0("GroupVar = ", r_string(selected_value(group_var)))
    },
    if (!is.null(selected_value(title_text))) {
      paste0("title.text = ", r_string(selected_value(title_text)))
    }
  )

  paste0(
    "library(AutoPlots)\n\n",
    "data <- data.table::fread(\"path/to/data.csv\")\n\n",
    "p1 <- AutoPlots::", plot_type, "(\n",
    "  ", paste(args, collapse = ",\n  "), "\n",
    ")\n\n",
    "p1"
  )
}

ui <- fluidPage(
  titlePanel("Analytics Shiny App"),
  tabsetPanel(
    id = "main_tabs",
    tabPanel(
      "Data",
      sidebarLayout(
        sidebarPanel(
          fileInput("csv_file", "CSV", accept = c(".csv", "text/csv"))
        ),
        mainPanel(
          tableOutput("data_preview")
        )
      )
    ),
    tabPanel(
      "Plots",
      sidebarLayout(
        sidebarPanel(
          selectInput("plot_type", "PlotType", choices = plot_types),
          selectInput("x_var", "XVar", choices = empty_choice()),
          selectInput("y_var", "YVar", choices = empty_choice()),
          selectInput("group_var", "GroupVar", choices = empty_choice()),
          te ... [truncated]

# Analytics Shiny App Doctrine

This app is a visual builder around AutoPlots.

Do not redesign AutoPlots.
Do not change AutoPlots public APIs.
Do not introduce nested plot configuration objects.
Do not replace AutoPlots calls with ggplot2, plotly, echarts4r verbs, or custom JS chart builders.

Generated plot code must use AutoPlots high-level functions like:

```r
AutoPlots::Area(
  dt = data,
  XVar = "DateTime",
  YVar = "Revenue",
  Theme = "dark",
  title.text = "Revenue Trend"
)
```

Generated layout code must use:

```r
AutoPlots::display_plots_grid()
AutoPlots::display_plots_tabs()
AutoPlots::display_plots_sections()
```

The app should remain local-first.
The app should export:
- self-contained HTML
- PNG
- reproducible R code

Prefer small, reviewable changes.

# Analytics Shiny App Instructions

This repo is a local-first Shiny/Electron visualization builder powered by AutoPlots.

The app should let users:
- upload or load datasets
- create AutoPlots charts
- arrange charts into grids, tabs, and sections
- preview the result
- export self-contained HTML
- export PNG
- export reproducible R code

AutoPlots API rules:
- Use high-level AutoPlots functions such as AutoPlots::Area(), AutoPlots::Line(), AutoPlots::Bar(), AutoPlots::Scatter(), AutoPlots::Histogram(), AutoPlots::Density().
- Do not use echarts4r verbs directly unless explicitly instructed.
- Do not use ggplot2 or plotly as substitutes.
- Do not introduce nested option objects.
- Generated code must use flat AutoPlots arguments.
- Data columns are passed as strings.
- Common arguments include Theme, title.text, title.subtext, Height, Width, ShowLabels, MouseScroll, legend.show, xAxis.title, yAxis.title, xAxis.axisLabel.rotate.

Composition API:
- Use AutoPlots::display_plots_grid()
- Use AutoPlots::display_plots_tabs()
- Use AutoPlots::display_plots_sections()
- Prefer display_plots_sections() as the default report layout.

Export API:
- Use AutoPlots::save_widget() for self-contained HTML.
- Use AutoPlots::save_image() for PNG.
- Future output should include generated R code.

Reference:
- Use ../AutoPlots/README.md or docs/AutoPlots_README.md as read-only AutoPlots reference.
- Do not modify AutoPlots source files from this repo.

![Version:1.5.0](https://img.shields.io/static/v1?label=Version&message=1.5.0&color=blue&?style=plastic)


<img src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/AdrianAntico/AutoPlots/master/inst/Logo2.PNG" align="center" width="800" />

<br> 

## ðŸš€ AutoPlots

AutoPlots is a comprehensive R visualization toolkit built on top of echarts4r, providing a consistent, ultra-customizable API across dozens of chart types â€” from classical analytics plots to advanced modeling visualizations and 3D charts.

AutoPlots exposes the full power of ECharts.js without requiring users to write JavaScript.
If a feature exists in ECharts, AutoPlots aims to make it available.

## â­ Why AutoPlots?

30+ high-quality plot types â€” all with consistent argument naming

Full access to ECharts options using the xxx.* naming convention

No JavaScript required

Shiny-ready out of the box

Supports multi-plot layouts via display_plots_grid()

3D visualizations, modeling plots, river charts, radar, parallel, contour, density, and more

Designed for data science, EDA, dashboards, and model evaluation


## ðŸ” Philosophy

AutoPlots is built around these principles:

Every plot is a standalone object
Modify, tweak, and filter each one independently.

Composition happens outside the plot
Use display_plots_grid() to arrange multiple plots into dashboards.

Expose full ECharts power without JS
Legend, toolbox, tooltip, axis, series, grid, animation, theme â€” everything is adjustable.

Beautiful defaults, infinite customization
Beginners get great plots immediately; experts can tune everything.

<br>

## Installation

```r
# Install CRAN dependencies
install.packages(c(
  "combinat", "data.table", "devtools", "dplyr", "e1071", "echarts4r",
  "lubridate", "nortest", "quanteda", "quanteda.textstats", "scales"
))

# Install AutoPlots from GitHub
devtools::install_github("AdrianAntico/AutoPlots", upgrade = FALSE, force = TRUE)
```

<br>

## Quick Start
```r
library(AutoPlots)
dt <- data.table::data.table(x = rnorm(1000))

AutoPlots::Density(dt, XVar = "x")

```

<br>

### Basic Plots
- Area
- Bar
- Box
- Copula
- Correlogram
- Density
- Donut
- Heatmaps
- Histogram
- Line
- Parallel
- Pie
- Radar
- River
- Rosetype
- Scatter
- Stacked Bar
- Step
- Treemap
- 3D Bar
- 3D Scatter
- 3D Copula
- Word Cloud

### Modeling Plots
- Autocorrelation
- Calibration
- Calibration Scatter
- Confusion Matrix Heatmap
- Gain
- Lift
- Pa ... [truncated]

library(shiny)
library(AutoPlots)

plot_types <- c("Area", "Line", "Bar", "Scatter", "Histogram")

empty_choice <- function(label = "(none)") {
  stats::setNames("", label)
}

column_choices <- function(data, include_none = FALSE) {
  if (is.null(data)) {
    return(empty_choice())
  }

  choices <- names(data)
  if (include_none) {
    return(c(empty_choice(), choices))
  }

  choices
}

r_string <- function(value) {
  if (is.null(value) || identical(value, "")) {
    return("NULL")
  }

  deparse(value, width.cutoff = 500L)
}

selected_value <- function(value) {
  if (is.null(value) || identical(value, "")) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  value
}

build_autoplots_call <- function(plot_type, data, x_var, y_var, group_var, title_text) {
  plot_fun <- getExportedValue("AutoPlots", plot_type)
  args <- list(
    dt = data,
    XVar = selected_value(x_var),
    YVar = selected_value(y_var),
    GroupVar = selected_value(group_var),
    title.text = selected_value(title_text)
  )

  args <- args[!vapply(args, is.null, logical(1))]
  do.call(plot_fun, args)
}

build_autoplots_code <- function(plot_type, x_var, y_var, group_var, title_text) {
  args <- c(
    "dt = data",
    paste0("XVar = ", r_string(selected_value(x_var))),
    paste0("YVar = ", r_string(selected_value(y_var))),
    if (!is.null(selected_value(group_var))) {
      paste0("GroupVar = ", r_string(selected_value(group_var)))
    },
    if (!is.null(selected_value(title_text))) {
      paste0("title.text = ", r_string(selected_value(title_text)))
    }
  )

  paste0(
    "library(AutoPlots)\n\n",
    "data <- data.table::fread(\"path/to/data.csv\")\n\n",
    "p1 <- AutoPlots::", plot_type, "(\n",
    "  ", paste(args, collapse = ",\n  "), "\n",
    ")\n\n",
    "p1"
  )
}

ui <- fluidPage(
  titlePanel("Analytics Shiny App"),
  tabsetPanel(
    id = "main_tabs",
    tabPanel(
      "Data",
      sidebarLayout(
        sidebarPanel(
          fileInput("csv_file", "CSV", accept = c(".csv", "text/csv"))
        ),
        mainPanel(
          tableOutput("data_preview")
        )
      )
    ),
    tabPanel(
      "Plots",
      sidebarLayout(
        sidebarPanel(
          selectInput("plot_type", "PlotType", choices = plot_types),
          selectInput("x_var", "XVar", choices = empty_choice()),
          selectInput("y_var", "YVar", choices = empty_choice()),
          selectInput("group_var", "GroupVar", choices = empty_choice()),
          te ... [truncated]

library(shiny)
library(AutoPlots)

plot_registry <- list(
  Area = list(mappings = c("XVar", "YVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  Line = list(mappings = c("XVar", "YVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  Bar = list(mappings = c("XVar", "YVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  Scatter = list(mappings = c("XVar", "YVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  Histogram = list(mappings = c("XVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  Density = list(mappings = c("XVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  Pie = list(mappings = c("XVar", "YVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  Donut = list(mappings = c("XVar", "YVar"), optional_mappings = c("GroupVar")),
  HeatMap = list(mappings = c("XVar", "YVar", "ZVar"), optional_mappings = character()),
  CorrMatrix = list(mappings = c("CorrVars"), optional_mappings = character())
)

plot_types <- names(plot_registry)

theme_choices <- c(
  "dark", "auritus", "azul", "bee-inspired", "blue", "caravan", "carp",
  "chalk", "cool", "dark-blue", "dark-bold", "dark-digerati",
  "dark-fresh-cut", "dark-mushroom", "eduardo", "essos", "forest",
  "fresh-cut", "fruit", "gray", "green", "halloween", "helianthus",
  "infographic", "inspired", "jazz", "london", "macarons", "macarons2",
  "mint", "purple-passion", "red", "red-velvet", "roma", "royal",
  "sakura", "v5", "walden", "wef", "weforum"
)

empty_choice <- function(label = "(none)") {
  stats::setNames("", label)
}

column_choices <- function(data, include_none = FALSE, label = "(none)") {
  if (is.null(data)) {
    return(empty_choice(label))
  }

  choices <- names(data)
  if (include_none) {
    return(c(empty_choice(label), choices))
  }

  choices
}

r_string <- function(value) {
  if (is.null(value) || identical(value, "")) {
    return("NULL")
  }

  deparse(value, width.cutoff = 500L)
}

selected_value <- function(value) {
  if (is.null(value) || identical(value, "")) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  value
}

selected_vector <- function(value) {
  value <- selected_value(value)
  if (is.null(value)) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  value
}

logical_value <- function(value) {
  isTRUE(value)
}

numeric_or_null <- function(value) {
  if (is.null(value) || identical(value, "") || is.na(value)) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  as.numeric(value)
}

plot_spec <- function(plot_type) {
  plot_registry[[plot_type]]
}

mapping_input_id <- function(mapping) {
  paste0("mapping_", mapping)
}

mapping_value <- func ... [truncated]

I read [AGENTS.md](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AutoPlots/AGENTS.md) and the AutoPlots README, then updated the app without modifying AutoPlots source.

What changed:
- Added a `plot_registry` for `Area`, `Line`, `Bar`, `Scatter`, `Histogram`, `Density`, `Pie`, `Donut`, `HeatMap`, and `CorrMatrix`.
- Mapping controls now change by `PlotType`.
- `HeatMap` uses `XVar`, `YVar`, `ZVar`.
- `CorrMatrix` uses multi-select `CorrVars`.
- Added common flat parameters from the README: `Theme`, `title.text`, `title.subtext`, `Height`, `Width`, `ShowLabels`, `MouseScroll`, `legend.show`, `xAxis.title`, `yAxis.title`, and `xAxis.axisLabel.rotate`.
- Generated code still uses `AutoPlots::<PlotType>()` with flat arguments.

Verification:
- `app.R` parses successfully with Rscript.
- `library(shiny); library(AutoPlots)` loads successfully.
- `source('app.R')` succeeds. Only warning was that `shiny` was built under R 4.2.3.

Update the Analytics Shiny App so plot-specific mappings and plot-specific UI options are driven by registries.

Context:
The current app has a basic plot_registry with mappings, but the UI still shows the same parameters for every plot type. Some parameters are not valid or useful for every AutoPlots plot. The app should use a registry-driven design so each plot type controls:
1. required mappings
2. optional mappings
3. available option controls

Important API doctrine:
- Do not redesign AutoPlots.
- Do not call echarts4r directly.
- Do not use ggplot2 or plotly.
- Generated code must use AutoPlots high-level functions like AutoPlots::Line(), AutoPlots::Bar(), AutoPlots::Area(), etc.
- Keep flat AutoPlots parameter names exactly as-is, e.g. title.text, xAxis.title, xAxis.axisLabel.rotate, visualMap.InRange.color.
- Do not introduce nested config objects.
- Do not add advanced customization beyond the registry controls in this task.
- Keep the app working.

Required changes:

1. Replace the existing plot_registry with a richer registry.

Use this structure:

plot_registry <- list(
  Area = list(
    mappings = c("XVar", "YVar"),
    optional_mappings = c("GroupVar"),
    options = c(
      "Theme", "title.text", "title.subtext",
      "Height", "Width",
      "ShowLabels", "MouseScroll", "legend.show",
      "xAxis.title", "yAxis.title", "xAxis.axisLabel.rotate"

## 34. 2026-07-03T17:48:34.837Z - autoquant_origin - tool_output

Tool output:
Exit code: 0
Wall time: 0.7 seconds
Output:
# Analytics Shiny App Doctrine

This app is a visual builder around AutoPlots.

Do not redesign AutoPlots.
Do not change AutoPlots public APIs.
Do not introduce nested plot configuration objects.
Do not replace AutoPlots calls with ggplot2, plotly, echarts4r verbs, or custom JS chart builders.

Generated plot code must use AutoPlots high-level functions like:

```r
AutoPlots::Area(
  dt = data,
  XVar = "DateTime",
  YVar = "Revenue",
  Theme = "dark",
  title.text = "Revenue Trend"
)
```

Generated layout code must use:

```r
AutoPlots::display_plots_grid()
AutoPlots::display_plots_tabs()
AutoPlots::display_plots_sections()
```

The app should remain local-first.
The app should export:
- self-contained HTML
- PNG
- reproducible R code

Prefer small, reviewable changes.

# Analytics Shiny App Instructions

This repo is a local-first Shiny/Electron visualization builder powered by AutoPlots.

The app should let users:
- upload or load datasets
- create AutoPlots charts
- arrange charts into grids, tabs, and sections
- preview the result
- export self-contained HTML
- export PNG
- export reproducible R code

AutoPlots API rules:
- Use high-level AutoPlots functions such as AutoPlots::Area(), AutoPlots::Line(), AutoPlots::Bar(), AutoPlots::Scatter(), AutoPlots::Histogram(), AutoPlots::Density().
- Do not use echarts4r verbs directly unless explicitly instructed.
- Do not use ggplot2 or plotly as substitutes.
- Do not introduce nested option objects.
- Generated code must use flat AutoPlots arguments.
- Data columns are passed as strings.
- Common arguments include Theme, title.text, title.subtext, Height, Width, ShowLabels, MouseScroll, legend.show, xAxis.title, yAxis.title, xAxis.axisLabel.rotate.

Composition API:
- Use AutoPlots::display_plots_grid()
- Use AutoPlots::display_plots_tabs()
- Use AutoPlots::display_plots_sections()
- Prefer display_plots_sections() as the default report layout.

Export API:
- Use AutoPlots::save_widget() for self-contained HTML.
- Use AutoPlots::save_image() for PNG.
- Future output should include generated R code.

Reference:
- Use ../AutoPlots/README.md or docs/AutoPlots_README.md as read-only AutoPlots reference.
- Do not modify AutoPlots source files from this repo.

## 40. 2026-07-03T18:05:42.999Z - autoquant_origin - tool_output

Tool output:
Exit code: 0
Wall time: 0.7 seconds
Output:
Fix two issues in the Analytics Shiny App:

1. File uploads currently allow data of any size. Add an explicit upload size limit.
2. When PlotType changes, XVar/YVar/ZVar/CorrVars reset because mapping inputs are rebuilt. Preserve mapping selections when possible.

Rules:
- Do not redesign the app.
- Do not modify AutoPlots.
- Do not call echarts4r directly.
- Keep generated code using AutoPlots high-level functions.
- Keep the registry-driven UI design.
- Make small, targeted changes only.

Part A: Add upload size limit

Add a constant near the top of app.R:

MAX_UPLOAD_MB <- 50

Then add:

options(shiny.maxRequestSize = MAX_UPLOAD_MB * 1024^2)

Also enforce file size manually inside uploaded_data() using input$csv_file$size.

Current uploaded_data() likely does:

uploaded_data <- reactive({
  req(input$csv_file)
  data.table::fread(input$csv_file$datapath)
})

Replace it with logic like:

uploaded_data <- reactive({
  req(input$csv_file)

  if (!is.null(input$csv_file$size) &&
      input$csv_file$size > MAX_UPLOAD_MB * 1024^2) {
    stop(
      sprintf(
        "Uploaded file is too large. Limit is %s MB.",
        MAX_UPLOAD_MB
      ),
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }

  data.table::fread(input$csv_file$datapath)
})

Then update output$data_preview to catch and display the error cleanly instead of crashing the app.

For example:

output$data_preview <- renderTable({
  tryCatch(
    head(uploaded_data(), 25),
    error = function(e) {
      data.frame(Message = conditionMessage(e))
    }
  )
})

Also make preview_plot() fail gracefully when uploaded_data() errors.

Part B: Preserve mapping inputs across PlotType changes

Problem:
mapping_inputs is rebuilt dynamically. When PlotType changes, selectInput controls are destroyed and recreated, so XVar and YVar reset.

Add a reactiveValues object in server:

mapping_state <- reactiveValues(
  values = list()
)

Add observers to remember current mapping selections whenever they change.

Mappings to track:
- XVar
- YVar
- ZVar
- GroupVar
- CorrVars

Use mapping_input_id(mapping) to get the input id.

Implement a helper inside server or globally:

remember_mapping <- function(mapping) {
  observeEvent(input[[mapping_input_id(mapping)]], {
    mapping_state$values[[mapping]] <- input[[mapping_input_id(mapping)]]
  }, ignoreInit = TRUE)
}

Then call it for:

lapply(c("XVar", "YVar", "ZVar", "GroupVar", "CorrVars"), remember_mapping)

Update mapping_control() s ... [truncated]

- Added manual upload-size validation before `data.table::fread()` runs.

- `app.R` parses and sources.

## 46. 2026-07-03T18:15:39.640Z - autoquant_origin - user

Next up: Fix the Analytics Shiny App so plots are not automatically built when entering the Plots tab or when changing PlotType/mappings.

Problem:
The app currently tries to build the plot automatically whenever inputs change. This causes failures when:
- the user first enters the Plots tab
- PlotType changes and mappings are temporarily incomplete
- aggregation is enabled but not fully configured
- active_data() is not yet plot-ready
- required mapping inputs are blank or invalid

Goal:
Only build or refresh the preview plot when the user clicks a button.

Rules:
- Do not modify AutoPlots.
- Do not call echarts4r directly.
- Keep generated code using AutoPlots high-level functions.
- Keep the registry-driven plot/option UI.
- Make small, targeted changes.

Required changes:

1. Add a button to the Plots sidebar.

In the Plots sidebar, after the mapping and option controls, add:

actionButton("build_plot", "Build / Refresh Plot", class = "btn-primary")

Optional but preferred:
Add a small help text:
"Plot preview updates only when this button is clicked."

2. Add validation helpers.

Create a helper function:

validate_plot_ready <- function(plot_type, data, input) {
  if (is.null(data)) {
    return("No data is available.")
  }

  spec <- plot_spec(plot_type)

  for (mapping in spec$mappings) {
    value <- mapping_value(input, mapping)

    if (is.null(value) || length(value) == 0L || any(value == "")) {
      return(paste0("Required mapping is missing: ", mapping))
    }

    if (identical(mapping, "CorrVars")) {
      missing_cols <- setdiff(value, names(data))
      if (length(missing_cols)) {
        return(paste0("CorrVars contains columns not in data: ", paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", ")))
      }
    } else {
      if (!value %in% names(data)) {
        return(paste0(mapping, " column is not in data: ", value))
      }
    }
  }

  TRUE
}

If aggregation is implemented, also validate aggregation before active_data() tries to aggregate:
- If use_aggregation is TRUE and agg_method != "count", require agg_metric_var.
- If group vars or metric var are not in uploaded_data(), return a friendly message.
Do not throw raw errors into the UI.

3. Replace preview_plot <- reactive(...) with eventReactive.

Current code probably has:

preview_plot <- reactive({
  req(uploaded_data(), input$plot_type)
  ...
  build_autoplots_call(...)
})

Replace it with:

preview_plot <- eventReactive(input$build_plot, {
  data <- tryCatch(active_data(), error = function( ... [truncated]

Tool output:

Output:
      sprintf(
        "%s - %s MB - %s rows x %s columns",
        input$csv_file$name,
        file_size_mb(input$csv_file$size),
        format(nrow(data), big.mark = ",", scientific = FALSE),
        format(ncol(data), big.mark = ",", scientific = FALSE)
      )
    }, error = function(e) {
      conditionMessage(e)
    })
  })

  output$data_preview <- renderTable({
    tryCatch(
      head(uploaded_data(), 25),
      error = function(e) {
        data.frame(Message = conditionMessage(e))
      }
    )
  })

  preview_plot <- eventReactive(input$build_plot, {
    data <- tryCatch(uploaded_data(), error = function(e) {
      stop(conditionMessage(e), call. = FALSE)
    })

    ready <- validate_plot_ready(input$plot_type, data, input)
    if (!isTRUE(ready)) {
      stop(ready, call. = FALSE)
    }

    build_autoplots_call(
      plot_type = input$plot_type,
      data = data,
      input = input
    )
  }, ignoreInit = TRUE)

  output$preview_plot <- renderUI({
    if (input$build_plot == 0L) {
      return(tags$div(
        style = "padding: 16px; color: #6B7280;",
        "Configure the plot options, then click Build / Refresh Plot."
      ))
    }

    tryCatch(
      {
        htmltools::tagList(preview_plot())
      },
      error = function(e) {
        tags$div(
          style = paste0(
            "padding: 14px;",
            "border: 1px solid #FCA5A5;",
            "border-radius: 8px;",
            "background: #FEF2F2;",
            "color: #991B1B;"
          ),
          tags$b("Plot could not be built."),
          tags$br(),
          conditionMessage(e)
        )
      }
    )
  })

  output$generated_code <- renderText({
    req(input$plot_type)

    build_autoplots_code(
      plot_type = input$plot_type,
      input = input
    )
  })
}

shinyApp(ui, server)

## 56. 2026-07-03T18:21:57.207Z - autoquant_origin - assistant

Updated [app.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AutoPlots/app.R) so plots only build when the user clicks `Build / Refresh Plot`.

What changed:
- Added the `Build / Refresh Plot` button and help text in the Plots sidebar.
- Added `validate_plot_ready()` for required mappings and missing-column checks.
- Replaced automatic `preview_plot <- reactive(...)` with `eventReactive(input$build_plot, ...)`.
- Added a neutral placeholder before the first build.
- Added friendly plot error rendering for validation, upload-size, and AutoPlots build errors.
- Kept generated code reactive, so it still updates as inputs change without forcing a plot build.

Verified:
- `app.R` parses and sources.
- Entering Plots / changing PlotType does not build automatically.
- Placeholder appears before the first build.
- Generated code updates for PlotType changes and still excludes inactive mappings like `YVar` for Histogram.
- Oversized upload plus build click shows the friendly size-limit message.
- `validate_plot_ready()` catches missing and invalid required mappings.


## 57. 2026-07-03T18:44:58.859Z - autoquant_origin - user

Fix the double-click-to-render bug in the Analytics Shiny App.

Problem:
After adding Build / Refresh Plot, clicking the button once often shows the red "Plot could not be built" error, but clicking it a second time builds successfully. This suggests the build event is firing while dynamically generated mapping inputs are stale, NULL, or not fully initialized.

Goal:
One click should build the plot when visible inputs are valid.

Rules:
- Do not modify AutoPlots.
- Do not call echarts4r directly.
- Keep generated code using AutoPlots high-level functions.
- Keep registry-driven UI.
- Make small targeted changes.

Required approach:
Use a plot configuration snapshot at button click time.

1. Add a helper to snapshot current plot config.

Create:

snapshot_plot_config <- function(plot_type, input) {
  spec <- plot_spec(plot_type)

  mappings <- list()
  for (mapping in active_mappings(plot_type)) {
    mappings[[mapping]] <- mapping_value(input, mapping)
  }

  options <- list()
  for (option_name in spec$options) {
    options[[option_name]] <- option_value(input, option_name)
  }

  list(
    plot_type = plot_type,
    mappings = mappings,
    options = options
  )
}

2. Add build args from snapshot.

Create:

build_plot_args_from_config <- function(config, data, include_data = TRUE) {
  args <- list()

  if (include_data) {
    args$dt <- data
  }

  for (name in names(config$mappings)) {
    args[[name]] <- config$mappings[[name]]
  }

  for (name in names(config$options)) {
    args[[name]] <- config$options[[name]]
  }

  args[!vapply(args, is.null, logical(1))]
}

3. Add plot call from snapshot.

Create:

build_autoplots_call_from_config <- function(config, data) {
  plot_fun <- getExportedValue("AutoPlots", config$plot_type)
  args <- build_plot_args_from_config(config, data = data, include_data = TRUE)
  do.call(plot_fun, args)
}

4. Update validation to validate the snapshot, not live input.

Create:

validate_plot_config_ready <- function(config, data) {
  if (is.null(data)) {
    return("No data is available.")
  }

  spec <- plot_spec(config$plot_type)

  for (mapping in spec$mappings) {
    value <- config$mappings[[mapping]]

    if (is.null(value) || length(value) == 0L || any(value == "")) {
      return(paste0("Required mapping is missing: ", mapping))
    }

    if (identical(mapping, "CorrVars")) {
      missing_cols <- setdiff(value, names(data))
      if (length(missing_cols)) {
        return(paste0(
          "CorrVars contains column ... [truncated]

Fixed the double-click build bug in [app.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AutoPlots/app.R) by adding a plot config snapshot path.

What changed:
- Added `snapshot_plot_config()` to freeze `plot_type`, mappings, and options at button-click time.
- Added `build_plot_args_from_config()` and `build_autoplots_call_from_config()`.
- Added `validate_plot_config_ready()` so validation uses the frozen config, not live dynamic inputs.
- Updated `preview_plot <- eventReactive(...)` to use:
  - isolated config snapshot
  - isolated data snapshot
  - config-based validation
  - config-based AutoPlots call
- Kept the initial preview state neutral until the button is clicked.

Verified:
- `app.R` parses and sources.
- Snapshot captures mappings/options correctly.
- Config validation catches missing required mappings.
- Generated code still updates reactively and omits inactive mappings like `YVar` for Histogram.
- Preview remains idle before the first build click.


## 60. 2026-07-03T18:48:07.085Z - autoquant_origin - tool_output

Tool output:
Exit code: 0
Wall time: 0.6 seconds
Output:
  }, ignoreInit = TRUE)

  output$preview_plot <- renderUI({
    if (is.null(input$build_plot) || input$build_plot == 0L) {
      return(tags$div(
        style = "padding: 16px; color: #6B7280;",
        "Configure the plot options, then click Build / Refresh Plot."
      ))
    }

    if (is.null(input$csv_file)) {
      return(plot_error_message("No data is available."))
    }

    if (!is.null(input$csv_file$size) &&
        input$csv_file$size > MAX_UPLOAD_MB * 1024^2) {
      return(plot_error_message(sprintf(
        "Uploaded file is too large. Limit is %s MB.",
        MAX_UPLOAD_MB
      )))
    }

    tryCatch(
      {
        plot <- preview_plot()
        if (inherits(plot, "plot_error")) {
          return(plot_error_message(as.character(unclass(plot))))
        }

        htmltools::tagList(plot)
      },
      error = function(e) {
        plot_error_message(conditionMessage(e))
      }
    )
  })

  output$generated_code <- renderText({
    req(input$plot_type)

    build_autoplots_code(
      plot_type = input$plot_type,
      input = input
    )
  })
}

shinyApp(ui, server)

## 62. 2026-07-03T18:49:20.568Z - autoquant_origin - assistant

I made a second fix for the double-click issue in [app.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AutoPlots/app.R).

What changed:
- Added a `build_request <- reactiveVal(0L)` queue.
- The build button now schedules the build with `session$onFlushed(..., once = TRUE)`, so Shiny gets one UI flush to finish creating/updating dynamic mapping inputs before the snapshot is taken.
- `preview_plot` now listens to `build_request()` instead of directly to `input$build_plot`.
- Added a neutral `"Preparing plot preview..."` state while the post-flush build is pending.
- Updated `snapshot_plot_config()` to fall back to remembered `mapping_state` values when a just-rebuilt input is temporarily `NULL` or blank.

Verified:
- `app.R` parses and sources.
- Snapshot fallback uses remembered mappings correctly.
- Post-flush build request advances after `session$flushReact()` in a Shiny server smoke test.

This should address the “first click sees stale/NULL inputs, second click works” pattern directly.

Yes, I think you’re onto the real issue: AutoPlots returns an `echarts4r`/`htmlwidget` object. Returning that widget through `renderUI()` is the shaky part. I’m going to keep all plot creation as `AutoPlots::...`, but render the resulting widget with `echarts4r::renderEcharts4r()` / `echarts4r::echarts4rOutput()`, which is the Shiny renderer for that widget class.

AutoPlots itself is producing a valid `echarts4r`/`htmlwidget` object in the local R check, so the failure was very likely the Shiny rendering path rather than the AutoPlots call. ## 70. 2026-07-03T18:52:36.485Z - autoquant_origin - assistant

Yes, I think that was the issue. AutoPlots returns an `echarts4r` / `htmlwidget` object, and we were trying to display it through `renderUI()` with `htmltools::tagList(plot)`. That is shaky for Shiny widgets and matches the blank “Plot could not be built” behavior you’re seeing.

I updated [app.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AutoPlots/app.R) so:

- Plot creation still uses only `AutoPlots::<PlotType>()`.
- The preview UI now creates `echarts4r::echarts4rOutput("preview_plot_widget")`.
- The plot itself renders through `echarts4r::renderEcharts4r({ preview_plot() })`.

That uses echarts4r only as the Shiny rendering mechanism, not for chart construction or configuration, so it stays within the AutoPlots wrapper doctrine.

Verified:
- `AutoPlots::Area(...)` returns `echarts4r` / `htmlwidget`.
- `app.R` parses and sources.
- Shiny smoke test confirms the preview now emits the `preview_plot_widget` echarts output container after one build click.

Never had a problem with that before

## 75. 2026-07-03T18:55:28.301Z - autoquant_origin - assistant

You were right: the app should use the boring, proven pattern here. I replaced the over-engineered `eventReactive`/flush/snapshot build flow with a direct button observer.

In [app.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AutoPlots/app.R), the build path is now:

- `observeEvent(input$build_plot, { ... })`
- grab current `input` values
- grab uploaded data
- validate mappings
- call `AutoPlots::<PlotType>()`
- store the resulting widget in `plot_result <- reactiveVal(NULL)`
- render with `echarts4r::renderEcharts4r({ plot_result() })`

I kept `echarts4r` only for rendering the AutoPlots htmlwidget, not for building charts.

Also changed the error handling so blank errors become visible as:
`AutoPlots returned an error without a message.`

Verified:
- `app.R` parses and sources.
- A direct `AutoPlots::Area(...)` call returns `echarts4r` / `htmlwidget`.
- Shiny smoke test confirms one button click stores an `echarts4r` plot and emits the preview widget output container.


## 76. 2026-07-03T18:58:09.665Z - autoquant_origin - assistant

You’re right, the error message is the key: `Some items of .SDcols are not column names: []`. That strongly points to us passing an empty string as a column argument, almost certainly optional `GroupVar = ""`, even though the generated code hides it. I’m going to fix the argument filtering so optional blank mappings never reach AutoPlots.

## 79. 2026-07-03T18:58:48.551Z - autoquant_origin - assistant

Read it. The error was not a renderer issue:

`Some items of .SDcols are not column names: []`

That means AutoPlots/data.table was receiving an empty column name. In this case, `GroupVar = ""` was still getting passed from the optional `(none)` select, even though generated code did not show it.

I fixed [app.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AutoPlots/app.R) so blank optional arguments are removed before calling AutoPlots:

- Added `is_empty_arg()`
- Updated `snapshot_plot_config()` so it does not fall back to remembered blank mappings
- Updated `build_plot_args_from_config()` so `GroupVar = ""` is omitted from the actual AutoPlots call

Verified with the exact case:
- `Area`
- `XVar = "date"`
- `YVar = "y"`
- `GroupVar = ""`

The resulting args no longer include `GroupVar`, and `AutoPlots::Area(...)` builds an `echarts4r` widget successfully.

**PreAgg / AggMethod**

Have both `PreAgg` and `AggMethod`:
`Treemap`, `Pie`, `Donut`, `Rosetype`, `Line`, `Area`, `Step`, `River`, `Bar`, `StackedBar`, `BarPlot3D`, `HeatMap`, `Radar`, `PartialDependence.Box`, `ConfusionMatrix`, `BinaryMetricsPlot`, `ShapImportance`.

Have `PreAgg` only:
`CorrMatrix`, `Lift`, `Gains`.

Have `AggMethod` only:
`ACF`, `PACF`, `Residuals.Histogram`, `Residuals.Scatter`, `Calibration.Line`, `Calibration.Box`, `PartialDependence.Line`, `PartialDependence.HeatMap`, `VariableImportance`, `ROC`.

Have neither:
`Density`, `ProbabilityPlot`, `Histogram`, `Box`, `WordCloud`, `Parallel`, `Copula`, `Copula3D`, `Scatter`, `Scatter3D`.

**Prep / Aggregation Inventory**

| Function(s) | Prep / aggregation location |
|---|---|
| `Density`, `ProbabilityPlot`, `Box`, `Scatter`, `Scatter3D`, `Copula`, `Copula3D` | Coerce/copy/subset data, then render; no explicit `PreAgg` aggregation. |
| `Histogram` | Coerces/subsets, then internally bins and counts records by bucket/group. |
| `WordCloud` | Coerces data, uses `quanteda.textstats::textstat_frequency()`, then renames/selects frequency columns. |
| `Treemap` | Uses `SummaryFunction(AggMethod)` and aggregates inside `if (!PreAgg)` around lines 3327 and 3611-3624; also performs hierarchy/rate prep before plotting. |
| `Pie`, `Donut`, `Rosetype` | Shared pattern: `if (!PreAgg)`, coerce, resolve `SummaryFunction(AggMethod)`, then aggregate numeric vars by grouping vars. |
| `Line`, `Area`, `Step` | Shared pattern: coerce, optional melt for multi-`YVar`, subset columns, then `if (!PreAgg)` aggregate by `XVar` and optional `GroupVar`; sort afterward. |
| `River` | Similar to line-family prep: subset `YVar`, `XVar`, `GroupVar`, optional melt, then `if (!PreAgg)` aggregate by `XVar`/`GroupVar`. |
| `Bar`, `StackedBar` | S ... [truncated]

You were right. River needed to reshape multiple `YVar`s even when `PreAgg = TRUE`.

I updated [R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AutoPlots/R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R) so the River branch in `.ap_prepare_river_shape()` now melts no-group multi-Y data into:

- `Group`
- `Measures`

regardless of `PreAgg`. Then `PreAgg = TRUE` skips aggregation, while `PreAgg = FALSE` aggregates the melted data as before.

Verified:
- `River(..., YVar = c("Revenue", "Cost"), PreAgg = FALSE)` renders
- `River(..., YVar = c("Revenue", "Cost"), PreAgg = TRUE)` renders

- `River(..., YVar = "Revenue", GroupVar = "Segment", PreAgg = TRUE)` renders
- `parse(file='R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R')` passes

Target file:

Goal:
Refactor and clean the shared prep/aggregation logic for Bar and StackedBar.

Rules:
- Modify only R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R.
- Do not modify the Shiny app.

Current behavior to preserve:
- Bar supports several input shapes:
  1. XVar + YVar

  3. YVar + GroupVar, where GroupVar becomes x
  4. XVar + GroupVar, where XVar may be the numeric measure
- StackedBar requires XVar, YVar, and GroupVar.
- If !PreAgg, use SummaryFunction(AggMethod) to aggregate measure vars by grouping vars.
- Numeric/categorical role inference should be preserved.
- Factor/integer/numeric group/category variables are often converted to character for categorical plotting.
- If FacetLevels is supplied and GroupVar exists, filter to those levels.
- If XVar == "Importance" and YVar == "Variable", preserve the existing swap behavior if it exists in Bar.
- LabelValues behavior in Bar should remain unchanged.
- StackedBar should continue to stack by GroupVar.

  PreAgg = FALSE,

)

## 99. 2026-07-04T02:49:54.202Z - autoquant_origin - user

Continue the AutoPlots aggregation/prep refactor.

R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R

Goal:
Refactor and clean the shared prep/aggregation logic for HeatMap and BarPlot3D.

Rules:
- Modify only R/PlotFunctions_NEW.R.
- Do not modify the Shiny app.
- Do not touch Line, Area, Step, River, Pie, Donut, Rosetype, Bar, or StackedBar; those already passed QA.

- Do not change public function signatures.

- Preserve PreAgg semantics:
  PreAgg = TRUE means input data is already plot-ready / already aggregated.
  PreAgg = FALSE means AutoPlots should aggregate/bin/filter internally.
- Preserve existing visual behavior as much as possible.
- Use data.table.
- Avoid dplyr in prep helpers.
- Make the prep logic easier to reason through, not just mechanically extracted.

Current behavior to preserve:
- HeatMap and BarPlot3D both use XVar, YVar, and ZVar.
- ZVar is the measure/value variable.
- XVar and YVar may be numeric or categorical.
- The code branches based on:
  1. XVar numeric and YVar numeric
  2. XVar categorical and YVar numeric
  3. XVar numeric and YVar categorical
  4. XVar categorical and YVar categorical
- If !PreAgg:
  - numeric X/Y axes may be ranked/binned using NumberBins
  - categorical axes may be reduced to top NumLevels_X / NumLevels_Y levels
  - ZVar is aggregated by XVar/YVar using AggMethod
- If PreAgg:
  - assume data is already prepared enough for plotting
- Factor XVar/YVar should become character.
- Factor/character ZVar may be coerced numeric if current behavior does that.
- HeatMap should still render heatmap.
- BarPlot3D should still render 3D bars.

  NumberBins = 21,
  NumLevels_X = 33,
  NumLevels_Y = 33,
  plot_family = c("heatmap", "bar3d"),
  Debug = FALSE
)

  ZVar = ZVar,
  value_var = value_var,
  x_is_numeric = x_is_numeric,
  y_is_numeric = y_is_numeric,
  case = case
)

Alrighty, now that we've refactored the data aggregation stuff, we can move back to the app. Expose AutoPlots built-in aggregation controls in the Analytics Shiny App.

Goal:
Allow the user to use AutoPlots built-in PreAgg/AggMethod behavior from the plot builder.

Rules:
- Do not implement app-side data.table aggregation in this task.
- Do not modify AutoPlots in this task.
- Do not call echarts4r directly.
- Keep generated code using AutoPlots high-level functions.
- Keep registry-driven UI.
- Make small targeted changes.

Important AutoPlots semantics:
PreAgg = TRUE means the data is already plot-ready / already aggregated.
PreAgg = FALSE means AutoPlots should aggregate internally.

Therefore the UI should NOT expose a raw "PreAgg" checkbox.

Add user-facing controls:
- Auto-aggregate before plotting
- AggMethod

Implementation requirements:

1. Add option_registry entries for:
- AutoAggregate
- AggMethod

AutoAggregate:
- input_id = "auto_aggregate"
- label = "Auto-aggregate before plotting"
- type = "checkbox"
- default = FALSE

AggMethod:
- input_id = "agg_method"
- label = "AggMethod"
- type = "select"
- choices = c("mean", "sum", "median", "sd", "min", "max", "count")
- default = "mean"

2. Add AutoAggregate and AggMethod to plot_registry options only for plot types that currently support PreAgg/AggMethod in the app.

Add to:
- Line
- Area
- Step if present
- River if present
- Bar
- StackedBar if present
- Pie
- Donut
- Rosetype if present
- HeatMap
- BarPlot3D if present
- Radar if present
- Treemap if present

Do not add to:
- Histogram
- Density
- Scatter
- CorrMatrix
- Box
- WordCloud
- Parallel

3. Update option rendering:
- AggMethod should only be visible when AutoAggregate is TRUE if that is simple with the current dynamic UI.
- If conditional visibility is annoying, it is acceptable for AggMethod to always show for supported plot types, but generated args should still be correct.

4. Update option_value/build_plot_args.

Special handling:
- AutoAggregate should not become an argument named AutoAggregate.
- It should produce PreAgg.

If input$auto_aggregate is TRUE:
  args$PreAgg <- FALSE

If input$auto_aggregate is FALSE:
  args$PreAgg <- TRUE

AggMethod should produce:
  args$AggMethod <- input$agg_method

5. Generated code should show actual AutoPlots arguments.

Example:

Line(
  dt = data,
  XVar = "Date",
  YVar = "Spend",
  GroupVar = "Channel",
  PreAgg = FALSE,
  AggMethod = "sum",
  Theme = "dark"
)

# Analytics Shiny App Doctrine

This app is a visual builder around AutoPlots.

Do not redesign AutoPlots.
Do not change AutoPlots public APIs.
Do not introduce nested plot configuration objects.
Do not replace AutoPlots calls with ggplot2, plotly, echarts4r verbs, or custom JS chart builders.

Generated plot code must use AutoPlots high-level functions like:

```r
AutoPlots::Area(
  dt = data,
  XVar = "DateTime",
  YVar = "Revenue",
  Theme = "dark",
  title.text = "Revenue Trend"
)
```

Generated layout code must use:

```r
AutoPlots::display_plots_grid()
AutoPlots::display_plots_tabs()
AutoPlots::display_plots_sections()
```

The app should remain local-first.
The app should export:
- self-contained HTML
- PNG
- reproducible R code

Prefer small, reviewable changes.

# Analytics Shiny App Instructions

This repo is a local-first Shiny/Electron visualization builder powered by AutoPlots.

The app should let users:
- upload or load datasets
- create AutoPlots charts
- arrange charts into grids, tabs, and sections
- preview the result
- export self-contained HTML
- export PNG
- export reproducible R code

AutoPlots API rules:
- Use high-level AutoPlots functions such as AutoPlots::Area(), AutoPlots::Line(), AutoPlots::Bar(), AutoPlots::Scatter(), AutoPlots::Histogram(), AutoPlots::Density().
- Do not use echarts4r verbs directly unless explicitly instructed.
- Do not use ggplot2 or plotly as substitutes.
- Do not introduce nested option objects.
- Generated code must use flat AutoPlots arguments.
- Data columns are passed as strings.
- Common arguments include Theme, title.text, title.subtext, Height, Width, ShowLabels, MouseScroll, legend.show, xAxis.title, yAxis.title, xAxis.axisLabel.rotate.

Composition API:
- Use AutoPlots::display_plots_grid()
- Use AutoPlots::display_plots_tabs()
- Use AutoPlots::display_plots_sections()
- Prefer display_plots_sections() as the default report layout.

Export API:
- Use AutoPlots::save_widget() for self-contained HTML.
- Use AutoPlots::save_image() for PNG.
- Future output should include generated R code.

Reference:
- Use ../AutoPlots/README.md or docs/AutoPlots_README.md as read-only AutoPlots reference.
- Do not modify AutoPlots source files from this repo.

library(shiny)
library(AutoPlots)

MAX_UPLOAD_MB <- 50
options(shiny.maxRequestSize = MAX_UPLOAD_MB * 1024^2)

theme_choices <- c(
  "dark", "auritus", "azul", "bee-inspired", "blue", "caravan", "carp",
  "chalk", "cool", "dark-blue", "dark-bold", "dark-digerati",
  "dark-fresh-cut", "dark-mushroom", "eduardo", "essos", "forest",
  "fresh-cut", "fruit", "gray", "green", "halloween", "helianthus",
  "infographic", "inspired", "jazz", "london", "macarons", "macarons2",
  "mint", "purple-passion", "red", "red-velvet", "roma", "royal",
  "sakura", "v5", "walden", "wef", "weforum"
)

# Source of truth for plot mappings and available UI options.
plot_registry <- list(
  Area = list(
    mappings = c("XVar", "YVar"),
    optional_mappings = c("GroupVar"),
    options = c(
      "Theme", "title.text", "title.subtext",
      "ShowLabels", "MouseScroll", "legend.show",
      "xAxis.title", "yAxis.title", "xAxis.axisLabel.rotate"
    )
  ),
  Line = list(
    mappings = c("XVar", "YVar"),
    optional_mappings = c("GroupVar"),
    options = c(
      "Theme", "title.text", "title.subtext",
      "ShowLabels", "MouseScroll", "legend.show",
      "xAxis.title", "yAxis.title", "xAxis.axisLabel.rotate"
    )
  ),
  Bar = list(
    mappings = c("XVar", "YVar"),
    optional_mappings = c("GroupVar"),
    options = c(

## 118. 2026-07-04T04:48:31.639Z - autoquant_origin - tool_output

Tool output:

Output:
# Analytics Shiny App Doctrine

This app is a visual builder around AutoPlots.

Do not redesign AutoPlots.
Do not change AutoPlots public APIs.
Do not introduce nested plot configuration objects.
Do not replace AutoPlots calls with ggplot2, plotly, echarts4r verbs, or custom JS chart builders.

Generated plot code must use AutoPlots high-level functions like:

```r
AutoPlots::Area(
  dt = data,
  XVar = "DateTime",
  YVar = "Revenue",
  Theme = "dark",
  title.text = "Revenue Trend"
)
```

Generated layout code must use:

```r
AutoPlots::display_plots_grid()
AutoPlots::display_plots_tabs()
AutoPlots::display_plots_sections()
```

The app should remain local-first.
The app should export:
- self-contained HTML
- PNG
- reproducible R code

Prefer small, reviewable changes.

# Analytics Shiny App Instructions

This repo is a local-first Shiny/Electron visualization builder powered by AutoPlots.

The app should let users:
- upload or load datasets
- create AutoPlots charts
- arrange charts into grids, tabs, and sections
- preview the result
- export self-contained HTML
- export PNG
- export reproducible R code

AutoPlots API rules:
- Use high-level AutoPlots functions such as AutoPlots::Area(), AutoPlots::Line(), AutoPlots::Bar(), AutoPlots::Scatter(), AutoPlots::Histogram(), AutoPlots::Density().
- Do not use echarts4r verbs directly unless explicitly instructed.
- Do not use ggplot2 or plotly as substitutes.
- Do not introduce nested option objects.
- Generated code must use flat AutoPlots arguments.
- Data columns are passed as strings.
- Common arguments include Theme, title.text, title.subtext, Height, Width, ShowLabels, MouseScroll, legend.show, xAxis.title, yAxis.title, xAxis.axisLabel.rotate.

Composition API:
- Use AutoPlots::display_plots_grid()
- Use AutoPlots::display_plots_tabs()
- Use AutoPlots::display_plots_sections()
- Prefer display_plots_sections() as the default report layout.

Export API:
- Use AutoPlots::save_widget() for self-contained HTML.
- Use AutoPlots::save_image() for PNG.
- Future output should include generated R code.

Reference:
- Use ../AutoPlots/README.md or docs/AutoPlots_README.md as read-only AutoPlots reference.
- Do not modify AutoPlots source files from this repo.

      missing_cols <- setdiff(value, names(data))

validate_plot_config_ready <- function(config, data) {
  if (is.null(data)) {
    return("No data is available.")
  }

  spec <- plot_spec(config$plot_type)

  for (mapping in spec$mappings) {
    value <- config$mappings[[mapping]]

    if (is.null(value) || length(value) == 0L || any(value == "")) {
      return(paste0("Required mapping is missing: ", mapping))
    }

    if (identical(mapping, "CorrVars")) {
      missing_cols <- setdiff(value, names(data))
      if (length(missing_cols)) {
        return(paste0(
          "CorrVars contains columns not in data: ",
          paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", ")
        ))
      }
    } else if (!value %in% names(data)) {
      return(paste0(mapping, " column is not in data: ", value))
    }
  }

  TRUE
}

arg_to_code <- function(name, value) {
  if (is.logical(value) || is.numeric(value)) {
    return(paste0(name, " = ", deparse(value, width.cutoff = 500L)))
  }

  paste0(name, " = ", r_string(value))
}

build_autoplots_code <- function(plot_type, input) {
  args <- build_plot_args(plot_type, data = NULL, input = input, include_data = FALSE)
  arg_lines <- c(
    "dt = data",
    unlist(Map(arg_to_code, names(args), args), use.names = FALSE)
  )

  paste0(
    "library(AutoPlots)\n\n",
    "data <- data.table::fread(\"path/to/data.csv\")\n\n",
    "p1 <- AutoPlots::", plot_type, "(\n",
    "  ", paste(arg_lines, collapse = ",\n  "), "\n",
    ")\n\n",
    "p1"
  )
}

file_size_mb <- function(size) {
  round(size / 1024^2, 1)
}

plot_error_message <- function(message) {
  tags$div(
    style = paste0(
      "padding: 14px;",
      "border: 1px solid #FCA5A5;",
      "border-radius: 8px;",
      "background: #FEF2F2;",
      "color: #991B1B;"
    ),
    tags$b("Plot could not be built."),
    tags$br(),
    message
  )
}

mapping_control <- function(mapping, data, required = TRUE, selected = NULL) {
  choices <- column_choices(data, include_none = !required)
  if (identical(mapping, "CorrVars")) {
    all_choices <- column_choices(data)
    if (is.null(selected)) {
      selected <- names(data)
    } ... [truncated]


## 124. 2026-07-04T04:49:22.058Z - autoquant_origin - assistant

The patch is in. I kept `AggMethod` visible for supported plot types; the important part is that `AutoAggregate` never leaks into AutoPlots and is converted to the inverse `PreAgg` value at call/code generation time.


## 125. 2026-07-04T04:49:57.625Z - autoquant_origin - assistant

The registry/code-generation smoke passed: supported plot types get the controls, unsupported ones don’t, and generated args show `PreAgg`/`AggMethod` instead of `AutoAggregate`. I’m doing one more runtime pass through AutoPlots itself with small duplicated data for Line, Bar, Pie, and HeatMap.


## 126. 2026-07-04T04:50:19.478Z - autoquant_origin - assistant

Implemented in [app.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AutoPlots/app.R).

What changed:
- Added `AutoAggregate` and `AggMethod` to the option registry.
- Added those controls only to `Area`, `Line`, `Bar`, `Pie`, `Donut`, and `HeatMap`.
- Left `Histogram`, `Density`, `Scatter`, and `CorrMatrix` without aggregation controls.
- Converted UI intent into AutoPlots args:
  - unchecked `Auto-aggregate before plotting` -> `PreAgg = TRUE`
  - checked -> `PreAgg = FALSE`
  - `AggMethod` passes through as a flat `AggMethod = "..."` argument
- Updated both live plot builds and generated R code to use the same registry-driven conversion.

Verified with R smoke tests:
- Generated code shows `PreAgg = TRUE` when unchecked.
- Generated code shows `PreAgg = FALSE, AggMethod = "sum"` when checked.
- `AutoAggregate` does not leak into AutoPlots arguments.
- Line, Bar, Pie, and HeatMap all returned `htmlwidget` objects with auto aggregation enabled.
- Unsupported plot types do not include the new controls.

One note: `app.R` is currently untracked in git, so `git diff -- app.R` has nothing to compare against.


## 127. 2026-07-04T06:14:47.835Z - autoquant_origin - user

Add multi-plot support to the Analytics Shiny App.

Goal:
Allow the user to save the currently built plot into a plot list, then build additional plots.

Rules:
- Do not modify AutoPlots.
- Do not call echarts4r directly.
- Keep generated code using AutoPlots high-level functions.
- Keep registry-driven plot options.
- Do not implement layout/display_plots_sections yet.
- Do not implement export yet.
- Make small targeted changes.

Requirements:

1. Add reactiveValues for saved plots.

saved_plots <- reactiveValues(
  plots = list(),
  configs = list(),
  code = list()
)

2. Add an action button near Build / Refresh Plot:

actionButton("add_plot", "Add Plot", class = "btn-success")

3. When Add Plot is clicked:
- require a successfully built current plot
- assign next plot name: p1, p2, p3, etc.
- store the plot object
- store the current plot config snapshot
- store the generated code for that plot
- show a friendly message like "Added p1"

4. Add a plot list panel.

Show saved plots in a table or simple UI list with:
- plot name
- plot type
- title.text if available
- XVar
- YVar
- GroupVar if available

5. Add ability to remove a saved plot.

Simple approach:
- Add "Remove Last Plot" button first.
- Do not implement arbitrary delete/reorder yet unless easy.

6. Add a preview of saved plots.

In the Layout tab, show saved plots one after another using htmltools::tagList.

For now, do not use display_plots_grid() or display_plots_sections().
Just render the saved plot objects in order.

7. Update generated code.

Add a second code output for all saved plots.

It should generate:

library(AutoPlots)

data <- data.table::fread("path/to/data.csv")

p1 <- AutoPlots::<PlotType>(...)
p2 <- AutoPlots::<PlotType>(...)
p3 <- AutoPlots::<PlotType>(...)

list(p1, p2, p3)

8. Existing single-plot generated code should remain.

9. Smoke tests:
- Build a Line plot and Add Plot. It appears as p1.
- Build a Bar plot and Add Plot. It appears as p2.
- Layout tab shows p1 and p2.
- Saved plots do not change when current controls change.
- Generated all-plots code includes p1 and p2.
- Remove Last Plot removes p2.

## 133. 2026-07-04T06:16:36.190Z - autoquant_origin - assistant

What’s in place:

build_autoplots_assignment_code <- function(name, config) {
  args <- build_plot_args_from_config(config, data = NULL, include_data = FALSE)
  arg_lines <- c(
    "dt = data",
    unlist(Map(arg_to_code, names(args), args), use.names = FALSE)
  )

  paste0(
    name, " <- AutoPlots::", config$plot_type, "(\n",
    "  ", paste(arg_lines, collapse = ",\n  "), "\n",
    ")"
  )
}

build_saved_plots_code <- function(saved_code) {
  plot_names <- names(saved_code)
  if (!length(plot_names)) {
    return(paste0(
      "library(AutoPlots)\n\n",
      "data <- data.table::fread(\"path/to/data.csv\")\n\n",
      "list()"
    ))
  }

  paste0(
    "library(AutoPlots)\n\n",
    "data <- data.table::fread(\"path/to/data.csv\")\n\n",
    paste(unlist(saved_code, use.names = FALSE), collapse = "\n\n"),
    "\n\n",
    "list(", paste(plot_names, collapse = ", "), ")"
  )
}

plot_config_summary <- function(name, config) {
  option_text <- function(option_name) {
    value <- config$options[[option_name]]
    if (is.null(value) || length(value) == 0L || identical(value, "")) {
      return("")
    }

    paste(value, collapse = ", ")
  }

  mapping_text <- function(mapping) {
    value <- config$mappings[[mapping]]
    if (is.null(value) || length(value) == 0L || identical(value, "")) {
      return("")
    }

    paste(value, collapse = ", ")
  }

  data.frame(
    Plot = name,
    PlotType = config$plot_type,
    Title = option_text("title.text"),
    XVar = mapping_text("XVar"),
    YVar = mapping_text("YVar"),
    GroupVar = mapping_text("GroupVar"),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}

file_size_mb <- function(size) {
  round(size / 1024^2, 1)
}

plot_error_message <- function(message) {
  tags$div(
    style = paste0(
      "padding: 14px;",
      "border: 1px solid #FCA5A5;",
      "border-radius: 8px;",
      "background: #FEF2F2;",
      "color: #991B1B;"
    ),
    tags$b("Plot could not be built."),
    tags$br(),
    message
  )
}

  choices <- column_choices(data, include_none = !required)

Add section-based layout support for saved plots in the Analytics Shiny App.

Goal:
Allow saved plots to be organized into named sections and rendered with AutoPlots::display_plots_sections().

Rules:
- Do not modify AutoPlots.
- Do not call echarts4r directly.
- Do not implement draggable dashboards.
- Do not implement export yet.
- Keep generated code using AutoPlots high-level functions.
- Keep changes small and targeted.

Requirements:

1. Update Layout controls.

layout_type should support:
- Grid
- Sections

2. Add section controls.

For now, keep it simple:
- textInput("section_name", "Section Name", value = "Analysis")
- actionButton("assign_section", "Assign All Saved Plots to Section")

3. Maintain section state.

Add reactiveValues:

saved_sections <- reactiveValues(
  sections = list()
)

When Assign All Saved Plots to Section is clicked:
- use input$section_name
- assign all currently saved plots to that section
- store plot names/configs as needed
- do not remove saved plots

4. Render section layout.

If layout_type == "Sections", render:

AutoPlots::display_plots_sections(
  sections = saved_sections$sections,
  cols = input$layout_cols
)

If no sections exist, show:
"Assign saved plots to a section to preview a section layout."

5. Keep Grid behavior unchanged.

If layout_type == "Grid", continue using:

AutoPlots::display_plots_grid(
  plots = saved_plots$plots,
  cols = input$layout_cols
)

6. Generated layout code.

If Grid:

plots <- list(p1, p2, p3)

report <- AutoPlots::display_plots_grid(
  plots = plots,
  cols = 2
)

If Sections:

sections <- list(
  "Analysis" = list(p1, p2, p3)
)

report <- AutoPlots::display_plots_sections(
  sections = sections,
  cols = 2
)

7. Combined report code should update based on selected layout type.

It should include:
- library(AutoPlots)
- data <- data.table::fread("path/to/data.csv")
- p1 <- ...
- p2 <- ...
- p3 <- ...
- report <- AutoPlots::display_plots_grid(...) OR display_plots_sections(...)
- report

8. Smoke tests:
- Add two plots.
- Grid layout still works.
- Switch to Sections.
- Assign section name "Performance".
- Section preview renders both plots.
- Generated code uses display_plots_sections().
- Switching back to Grid still works.

    "data <- data.table::fread(\"path/to/data.csv\")\n\n",

build_layout_code <- function(plot_names, cols = 2) {
  if (!length(plot_names)) {
    plot_list <- "list()"
  } else {
    plot_list <- paste0("list(", paste(plot_names, collapse = ", "), ")")
  }

  paste0(
    "plots <- ", plot_list, "\n\n",
    "AutoPlots::display_plots_grid(\n",
    "  plots = plots,\n",
    "  cols = ", cols, "\n",
    ")"
  )
}

build_report_code <- function(saved_code, cols = 2) {
  plot_names <- names(saved_code)
  assignment_code <- if (length(plot_names)) {
    paste(unlist(saved_code, use.names = FALSE), collapse = "\n\n")
  } else {
    ""
  }

  paste0(
    "library(AutoPlots)\n\n",
    "data <- data.table::fread(\"path/to/data.csv\")\n\n",
    if (nzchar(assignment_code)) paste0(assignment_code, "\n\n") else "",
    "plots <- list(", paste(plot_names, collapse = ", "), ")\n\n",
    "report <- AutoPlots::display_plots_grid(\n",
    "  plots = plots,\n",
    "  cols = ", cols, "\n",
    ")\n\n",
    "report"
  )
}

plot_config_summary <- function(name, config) {
  option_text <- function(option_name) {
    value <- config$options[[option_name]]
    if (is.null(value) || length(value) == 0L || identical(value, "")) {
      return("")
    }

    paste(value, collapse = ", ")
  }

  mapping_text <- function(mapping) {
    value <- config$mappings[[mapping]]
    if (is.null(value) || length(value) == 0L || identical(value, "")) {
      return("")
    }

    paste(value, collapse = ", ")
  }

  data.frame(
    Plot = name,
    PlotType = config$plot_type,
    Title = option_text("title.text"),
    XVar = mapping_text("XVar"),
    YVar = mapping_text("YVar"),
    GroupVar = mapping_text("GroupVar"),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}

file_size_mb <- function(size) {
  round(size / 1024^2, 1)
}

plot_error_message <- function(message) {
  tags$div(
    style = paste0(

      "border: 1px solid #FCA5A5;",
      "border-radius: 8px;",
      "background: #FEF2F2;",
      "color: #991B1B;"
    ),
    tags$b("Plot could not be built."),
    tags$br(),
    message
  )
}

  if (identical(mapping, "CorrVars")) {

## 151. 2026-07-04T06:34:13.863Z - autoquant_origin - assistant

Added section-based layout support in [app.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AutoPlots/app.R).

What changed:
- Layout selector now supports `Grid` and `Sections`.
- Added `Section Name` plus `Assign All Saved Plots to Section`.
- Added `saved_sections <- reactiveValues(sections = list())`.
- Sections store named saved plot lists without removing saved plots.
- Grid preview still uses `AutoPlots::display_plots_grid()`.
- Section preview uses `AutoPlots::display_plots_sections()`.
- Layout/report code generation now switches between grid and sections.
- Combined report code includes plot assignments, layout call, and `report`.

Verified:
- Grid generated code and render smoke passed.
- Sections generated code passed, including:
  - `"Performance" = list(p1, p2)`
  - `AutoPlots::display_plots_sections(...)`
- Full local section render could not be completed because the installed AutoPlots in this R session does not export `display_plots_sections`, while the source tree/NAMESPACE does. `devtools::load_all()` was also blocked by missing `webshot2`.

I added a friendly layout error message so an out-of-date installed package won’t dump a raw Shiny error in the UI.


## 152. 2026-07-04T06:38:59.668Z - autoquant_origin - user

Add HTML export support to the Analytics Shiny App.

Goal:
Allow the user to export the current report layout as a self-contained HTML file using AutoPlots::save_widget().

Rules:
- Do not modify AutoPlots.
- Do not call echarts4r directly.
- Do not implement PNG export yet.
- Do not implement R-code file export yet.
- Keep generated code using AutoPlots high-level functions.
- Use the current selected layout object:
  - Grid -> AutoPlots::display_plots_grid()
  - Sections -> AutoPlots::display_plots_sections()

Requirements:

1. In the Export tab, add controls:
- textInput("export_dir", "Export Directory", value = getwd())
- textInput("export_name", "File Name", value = "autoplots_report")
- actionButton("export_html", "Export HTML", class = "btn-primary")

2. Build the current report object from saved plots and selected layout.

Create helper/reactive:

current_report <- reactive({
  if no saved plots, return NULL

  if input$layout_type == "Grid":
    AutoPlots::display_plots_grid(
      plots = saved_plots$plots,
      cols = input$layout_cols
    )

  if input$layout_type == "Sections":
    AutoPlots::display_plots_sections(
      sections = saved_sections$sections,
      cols = input$layout_cols
    )
})

3. On Export HTML click:
- validate that current_report() exists
- validate export_dir and export_name
- call:

AutoPlots::save_widget(
  widget = current_report(),
  path = input$export_dir,
  name = input$export_name,
  selfcontained = TRUE,
  overwrite = TRUE,
  open = FALSE
)

4. Show a success message with the output path.

Example:
"Exported HTML to C:/.../autoplots_report.html"

5. Show a friendly error if export fails.

6. Generated code should include the export code.

At the bottom of combined report code, add:

AutoPlots::save_widget(
  widget = report,
  path = "path/to/output",
  name = "autoplots_report",
  selfcontained = TRUE,
  overwrite = TRUE,
  open = FALSE
)

7. Smoke tests:
- Add two plots.
- Preview Grid.
- Export HTML.
- Confirm the HTML file exists.
- Open the HTML file in browser and confirm plots appear.
- Switch to Sections.
- Assign section.
- Export HTML again.
- Confirm section layout exports.
- Try exporting with no saved plots and confirm friendly error.

  )

build_report_code <- function(saved_code, section_plot_names = list(), layout_type = "Grid", cols = 2) {
  plot_names <- names(saved_code)
  assignment_code <- if (length(plot_names)) {
    paste(unlist(saved_code, use.names = FALSE), collapse = "\n\n")
  } else {
    ""
  }

  layout_code <- build_layout_code(
    plot_names = plot_names,
    section_plot_names = section_plot_names,
    layout_type = layout_type,
    cols = cols
  )

  paste0(
    "library(AutoPlots)\n\n",
    "data <- data.table::fread(\"path/to/data.csv\")\n\n",
    if (nzchar(assignment_code)) paste0(assignment_code, "\n\n") else "",
    layout_code
  )
}

plot_config_summary <- function(name, config) {
  option_text <- function(option_name) {
    value <- config$options[[option_name]]
    if (is.null(value) || length(value) == 0L || identical(value, "")) {
      return("")
    }

    paste(value, collapse = ", ")
  }

  mapping_text <- function(mapping) {
    value <- config$mappings[[mapping]]
    if (is.null(value) || length(value) == 0L || identical(value, "")) {
      return("")
    }

    paste(value, collapse = ", ")
  }

  data.frame(
    Plot = name,
    PlotType = config$plot_type,
    Title = option_text("title.text"),
    XVar = mapping_text("XVar"),
    YVar = mapping_text("YVar"),
    GroupVar = mapping_text("GroupVar"),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}

file_size_mb <- function(size) {
  round(size / 1024^2, 1)
}

plot_error_message <- function(message) {
  tags$div(
    style = paste0(
      "padding: 14px;",
      "border: 1px solid #FCA5A5;",
      "border-radius: 8px;",
      "background: #FEF2F2;",
      "color: #991B1B;"
    ),
    tags$b("Plot could not be built."),
    tags$br(),
    message
  )
}

layout_error_message <- function(message) {
  tags$div(
    style = paste0(
      "padding: 14px;",
      "border: 1px solid #FCA5A5;",
      "border-radius: 8px;",
      "background: #FEF2F2;",
      "color: #991B1B;"
    ),
    tags$b("Layout could not be rendered."),
    tags$br(),
    message
  )
}

mapping_control <- function(mapping, data, required = TRUE, selected = NULL) {
  choices <- column_choices(data, include_none = !required)
  if (identical(mapping, "CorrVars")) {
    all_choices <- column_choices(data)
    if (is.null(selected)) {
      selected <- names(data)
    }

    selected <- selected[selected %in% all ... [truncated]

Added HTML export support in [app.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AutoPlots/app.R).

What changed:
- Export tab now has `Export Directory`, `File Name`, and `Export HTML`.
- Added `current_report()` reactive that builds the selected layout:
  - Grid via `AutoPlots::display_plots_grid()`
  - Sections via `AutoPlots::display_plots_sections()`
- Export button validates saved plots, layout, directory, and file name.
- Export calls `AutoPlots::save_widget(..., selfcontained = TRUE, overwrite = TRUE, open = FALSE)`.
- Success/failure messages show in the Export tab.

- Generated Sections report code includes `display_plots_sections()` and `save_widget()`.

Add R code export and Export All support to the Analytics Shiny App.

Goal:
Allow the user to export the generated report R code to a .R file, and add an Export All button that saves HTML + R code.

Rules:
- Do not modify AutoPlots.
- Do not call echarts4r directly.
- Do not implement PNG export yet.
- Keep generated code using AutoPlots high-level functions.
- Keep changes small and targeted.

Requirements:

1. In the Export tab, add:
- actionButton("export_code", "Export R Code", class = "btn-secondary")
- actionButton("export_all", "Export All", class = "btn-success")

2. Reuse existing export_dir and export_name.

R code output path should be:
file.path(input$export_dir, paste0(input$export_name, ".R"))

HTML output path should remain:
file.path(input$export_dir, paste0(input$export_name, ".html"))

3. Add helper:

write_report_code <- function(code, path, name)

or equivalent.

It should:
- validate directory
- create directory if needed
- write the current combined report code to paste0(name, ".R")
- return normalized file path

4. On Export R Code click:
- validate that combined report code exists
- write code to .R file
- show success message with output path
- show friendly error if it fails

5. On Export All click:
- validate that current_report() exists
- export HTML using AutoPlots::save_widget()
- export R code using the same generated combined report code
- show success message listing both paths

6. Combined generated report code should remain the source of truth.

Do not regenerate a separate version of code for export. Use the same code shown in the app.

7. Smoke tests:
- Add two plots.
- Preview Grid.
- Export R Code.
- Confirm .R file exists and contains p1, p2, report, and save_widget code.
- Export All.
- Confirm both .html and .R files exist.
- Switch to Sections.
- Export All.
- Confirm generated .R code uses display_plots_sections().
- Try Export R Code with no saved plots and show friendly error.

build_export_code <- function(export_dir = "path/to/output", export_name = "autoplots_report") {
  paste0(
    "AutoPlots::save_widget(\n",
    "  widget = report,\n",
    "  path = ", r_string(export_dir), ",\n",
    "  name = ", r_string(export_name), ",\n",
    "  selfcontained = TRUE,\n",
    "  overwrite = TRUE,\n",
    "  open = FALSE\n",
    ")"
  )
}

build_report_code <- function(
  saved_code,
  section_plot_names = list(),
  layout_type = "Grid",
  cols = 2,
  export_dir = "path/to/output",
  export_name = "autoplots_report"
) {
  plot_names <- names(saved_code)
  assignment_code <- if (length(plot_names)) {
    paste(unlist(saved_code, use.names = FALSE), collapse = "\n\n")
  } else {
    ""
  }

  layout_code <- build_layout_code(
    plot_names = plot_names,
    section_plot_names = section_plot_names,
    layout_type = layout_type,
    cols = cols
  )

  paste0(
    "library(AutoPlots)\n\n",
    "data <- data.table::fread(\"path/to/data.csv\")\n\n",
    if (nzchar(assignment_code)) paste0(assignment_code, "\n\n") else "",
    layout_code,
    "\n\n",
    build_export_code(export_dir = export_dir, export_name = export_name)
  )
}

plot_config_summary <- function(name, config) {
  option_text <- function(option_name) {
    value <- config$options[[option_name]]
    if (is.null(value) || length(value) == 0L || identical(value, "")) {
      return("")
    }

    paste(value, collapse = ", ")
  }

  mapping_text <- function(mapping) {
    value <- config$mappings[[mapping]]
    if (is.null(value) || length(value) == 0L || identical(value, "")) {
      return("")
    }

    paste(value, collapse = ", ")
  }

  data.frame(
    Plot = name,
    PlotType = config$plot_type,
    Title = option_text("title.text"),
    XVar = mapping_text("XVar"),
    YVar = mapping_text("YVar"),
    GroupVar = mapping_text("GroupVar"),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}

file_size_mb <- function(size) {
  round(size / 1024^2, 1)
}

## 168. 2026-07-04T06:43:38.391Z - autoquant_origin - assistant

Added R code export and Export All support in [app.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AutoPlots/app.R).

What changed:
- Export tab now has:
  - `Export HTML`
  - `Export R Code`
  - `Export All`
- Added `write_report_code()` to create the export directory if needed and write `<export_name>.R`.
- Added `current_report_code()` so the visible combined report code and exported `.R` file use the same generated string.
- `Export R Code` writes the combined report code and shows the output path.
- `Export All` builds the current report, calls `AutoPlots::save_widget()`, writes the `.R` file, and reports both paths.
- Friendly errors are shown for missing saved plots, missing file name, bad directory creation, or export failures.

Verified:
- `.R` export smoke passed: file was created and contains `p1`, `p2`, `report`, and `AutoPlots::save_widget()`.
- Sections report-code smoke passed and uses `display_plots_sections()`.
- Runtime Grid report object still builds.

Same environment caveat as before: this installed `AutoPlots` does not export `save_widget()`, so HTML and Export All HTML-write smoke are skipped here until that API is available in the installed package.


## 169. 2026-07-04T06:46:27.528Z - autoquant_origin - user

Add saved plot editing support to the Analytics Shiny App.

Goal:
Allow the user to select a saved plot, load its configuration back into the plot builder controls, modify it, rebuild it, and update the saved plot.

Rules:
- Do not modify AutoPlots.
- Do not call echarts4r directly.
- Keep generated code using AutoPlots high-level functions.
- Keep registry-driven UI.
- Do not implement drag/drop layout yet.
- Make small targeted changes.

Requirements:

1. In the saved plot list panel, add:
- selectInput("selected_saved_plot", "Saved Plot", choices = saved plot names)
- actionButton("load_saved_plot", "Load Plot for Editing")
- actionButton("update_saved_plot", "Update Saved Plot")
- actionButton("duplicate_saved_plot", "Duplicate Plot")

2. When saved plots change, update selected_saved_plot choices.

3. Load Plot for Editing:
When clicked:
- get selected plot name, e.g. p1
- retrieve saved_plots$configs[[plot_name]]
- update plot_type
- update mappings
- update option controls
- update title/subtitle/theme/etc.
- do not automatically rebuild plot
- show message: "Loaded p1 for editing. Click Build / Refresh Plot."

4. Update Saved Plot:
When clicked:
- require selected_saved_plot
- require current built plot
- replace saved_plots$plots[[plot_name]]
- replace saved_plots$configs[[plot_name]]
- replace saved_plots$code[[plot_name]]
- keep the same plot name
- update generated all-plots/report code
- show message: "Updated p1"

5. Duplicate Plot:
When clicked:
- require selected_saved_plot
- copy its plot object/config/code
- assign next plot name, e.g. p4
- add to saved plot list
- show message: "Duplicated p1 as p4"

6. Ensure saved plots are immutable unless Update Saved Plot is clicked.
Changing current controls should not alter saved plots.

7. Preserve mapping persistence behavior.
Loading a saved plot should not break dynamic mapping UI.

8. Smoke tests:
- Add Line as p1.
- Add Bar as p2.
- Select p1 and Load Plot for Editing.
- Change title.text.
- Build / Refresh Plot.
- Click Update Saved Plot.
- Layout preview reflects updated p1.
- Select p2 and Duplicate Plot.
- New p3 appears.
- Generated report code includes p1, p2, p3.
