}

logical_value <- function(value) {
  isTRUE(value)
}

numeric_or_null <- function(value) {
  if (is.null(value) || identical(value, "") || is.na(value)) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  as.numeric(value)
}

plot_spec <- function(plot_type) {
  plot_registry[[plot_type]]
}

mapping_input_id <- function(mapping) {
  paste0("mapping_", mapping)
}

mapping_value <- function(input, mapping) {
  value <- input[[mapping_input_id(mapping)]]
  if (identical(mapping, "CorrVars")) {
    value <- selected_vector(value)
    if (is.null(value) || length(value) == 0L) {
      return(NULL)
    }

    return(value)
  }

  selected_value(value)
}

active_mappings <- function(plot_type) {
  spec <- plot_spec(plot_type)
  c(spec$mappings, spec$optional_mappings)
}

option_control <- function(option_name) {
  opt <- option_registry[[option_name]]
  if (is.null(opt)) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  switch(
    opt$type,
    select = selectInput(opt$input_id, opt$label, choices = opt$choices, selected = opt$default),
    text = textInput(opt$input_id, opt$label, value = opt$default),
    checkbox = checkboxInput(opt$input_id, opt$label, value = opt$default),
    numeric = numericInput(opt$input_id, opt$label, value = opt$default),
    NULL
  )
}

option_value <- function(input, option_name) {
  opt <- option_registry[[option_name]]
  if (is.null(opt)) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  value <- input[[opt$input_id]]
  switch(
    opt$type,
    checkbox = logical_value(value),
    numeric = numeric_or_null(value),
    selected_value(value)
  )
}

add_option_arg <- function(args, option_name, value) {
  if (identical(option_name, "AutoAggregate")) {
    args$PreAgg <- !isTRUE(value)
    return(args)
  }

  if (identical(option_name, "AggMethod")) {
    args$AggMethod <- value
    return(args)
  }

  args[[option_name]] <- value
  args
}

build_plot_args <- function(
  plot_type,
  data,
  input,
  include_data = TRUE
) {
  args <- list()
  if (include_data) {
    args$dt <- data
  }

  for (mapping in active_mappings(plot_type)) {
    args[[mapping]] <- mapping_value(input, mapping)
  }

  for (option_name in plot_spec(plot_type)$options) {
    args <- add_option_arg(args, option_name, option_value(input, option_name))
  }

  args[!vapply(args, is.null, logical(1))]
}

build_autoplots_call <- function(plot_type, data, input) {
  plot_fun <- getExportedValue("AutoPlots", plot_type)
  args <- build_plot_args(plot_type, data, input)
  do.call(plo ... [truncated]

## 173. 2026-07-04T06:49:36.790Z - autoquant_origin - assistant

Added saved plot editing support in [app.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AutoPlots/app.R).

What changed:
- Saved Plots panel now has:
  - saved plot selector
  - Load Plot for Editing
  - Update Saved Plot
  - Duplicate Plot
- Saved plot selector choices update as plots are added/removed/duplicated.
- Loading a saved plot restores `PlotType`, mappings, and registry-driven options back into the builder.
- Loading clears the current built plot so saved plots stay immutable until the user rebuilds and clicks Update.
- Updating replaces the selected saved plot object/config/code under the same name.
- Duplicating creates the next plot name, e.g. `p3`, and regenerates code with the new assignment name.
- If an updated plot is already in a section, that section reference is refreshed too.

Verified:
- Helper smoke passed for p-name generation and p1/p2/p3 report code.
- `shiny::testServer()` smoke passed:
  - added p1 Line
  - added p2 Bar
  - loaded p1 for editing and cleared current build
  - updated p1 with changed `title.text`
  - duplicated p2 as p3
  - generated code includes p1, p2, p3 correctly.

build_autoplots_assignment_code <- function(name, config) {
  args <- build_plot_args_from_config(config, data = NULL, include_data = FALSE)
  arg_lines <- c(
    "dt = data",
    unlist(Map(arg_to_code, names(args), args), use.names = FALSE)
  )

  paste0(
    name, " <- AutoPlots::", config$plot_type, "(\n",
    "  ", paste(arg_lines, collapse = ",\n  "), "\n",
    ")"
  )
}

next_plot_name <- function(plot_names) {
  if (!length(plot_names)) {
    return("p1")
  }

  plot_ids <- suppressWarnings(as.integer(sub("^p", "", plot_names)))
  plot_ids <- plot_ids[!is.na(plot_ids)]
  if (!length(plot_ids)) {
    return("p1")
  }

  paste0("p", max(plot_ids) + 1L)
}

build_saved_plots_code <- function(saved_code) {
  plot_names <- names(saved_code)
  if (!length(plot_names)) {
    return(paste0(
      "library(AutoPlots)\n\n",
      "data <- data.table::fread(\"path/to/data.csv\")\n\n",
      "list()"
    ))
  }

  paste0(
    "library(AutoPlots)\n\n",
    "data <- data.table::fread(\"path/to/data.csv\")\n\n",
    paste(unlist(saved_code, use.names = FALSE), collapse = "\n\n"),
    "\n\n",
    "list(", paste(plot_names, collapse = ", "), ")"
  )
}

plot_list_code <- function(plot_names) {
  if (!length(plot_names)) {
    return("list()")
  }

  paste0("list(", paste(plot_names, collapse = ", "), ")")
}

section_list_code <- function(section_plot_names) {
  section_names <- names(section_plot_names)
  if (!length(section_names)) {
    return("list()")
  }

  section_lines <- unlist(lapply(section_names, function(section_name) {
    paste0(
      "  ", r_string(section_name), " = ",
      plot_list_code(section_plot_names[[section_name]])
    )
  }), use.names = FALSE)

  paste0(
    "list(\n",
    paste(section_lines, collapse = ",\n"),
    "\n)"
  )
}

build_layout_code <- function(plot_names, section_plot_names = list(), layout_type = "Grid", cols = 2) {
  if (identical(layout_type, "Sections")) {
    return(paste0(
      "sections <- ", section_list_code(section_plot_names), "\n\n",
      "report <- AutoPlots::display_plots_sections(\n",
      "  sections = sections,\n",
      "  cols = ", cols, "\n",
      ")\n\n",
      "report"
    ))
  }

  paste0(
    "plots <- ", plot_list ... [truncated]

## 180. 2026-07-04T07:23:18.197Z - autoquant_origin - user

Add saved project state support to the Analytics Shiny App.

Goal:
Allow the user to save the current app/report-builder state to a project .rds file and load it later.

Rules:
- Do not modify AutoPlots.
- Do not call echarts4r directly.
- Do not implement a database.
- Do not implement cloud sync.
- Do not implement user accounts.
- Keep this local-first.
- Keep generated code using AutoPlots high-level functions.
- Make small targeted changes.

Project file format:
Use a single .rds file containing a named list.

Required saved state:
- app_version
- saved_at
- data_path if available
- data_name if available
- plot_configs
- plot_code
- plot_metadata
- layout_type
- layout_cols
- export_dir
- export_name

Optional saved state if already easy:
- current plot type
- current mapping selections
- current option values
- section names
- selected theme

Do not attempt to serialize htmlwidget plot objects as the primary saved artifact.

Important:
Saved project state should store plot configurations and regenerate plot objects on load.

Reason:
htmlwidget objects can be large, brittle, and may not deserialize cleanly across package/app changes.

Requirements:

1. Add Project controls.

In a Project tab or sidebar section, add:
- textInput("project_path", "Project File", value = file.path(getwd(), "autoplots_project.rds"))
- actionButton("save_project", "Save Project", class = "btn-primary")
- actionButton("load_project", "Load Project", class = "btn-secondary")

2. Save Project.

On save_project:
- collect the current project state into a named list
- include app_version = "0.1.0" or existing app version if defined
- include saved_at = Sys.time()
- include saved plot configs/code/metadata
- include layout/export settings
- saveRDS(project_state, input$project_path)
- show success message with normalized path

3. Load Project.

On load_project:
- readRDS(input$project_path)
- validate it is a list
- validate it contains expected fields
- restore saved plot configs/code/metadata
- restore layout/export settings
- restore project/data path info if available
- if data_path exists and file.exists(data_path), reload data automatically
- regenerate saved plot objects from saved configs using the current AutoPlots functions
- if data_path is missing or does not exist, load configs/metadata but show message:
  "Project loaded, but source data file was not found. Re-upload the data to rebuild plots."

4. Regenerating plots.

Create or reuse a helper that can rebuild a pl ... [truncated]

## 186. 2026-07-04T07:28:56.586Z - autoquant_origin - user

Add project validation and repair support to the Analytics Shiny App.

Goal:
Make loaded project files robust when source data, columns, saved plots, sections, or metadata are missing/stale.

Rules:
- Do not modify AutoPlots.
- Do not call echarts4r directly.
- Do not rewrite save/load from scratch.
- Do not eval generated code from project files.
- Continue saving configs, not htmlwidget objects.
- Keep changes small and targeted.

Requirements:

1. Add a project validation helper.

Create a helper like:

validate_project_state <- function(project_state, data = NULL)

It should return a structured list:

list(
  valid = TRUE/FALSE,
  errors = character(),
  warnings = character(),
  repaired_state = project_state
)

2. Validate top-level project structure.

Required fields:
- app_version
- saved_at
- plot_configs
- plot_code
- plot_metadata
- layout_type
- layout_cols
- export_dir
- export_name

If optional fields are missing, warn but do not fail.

3. Validate saved plot consistency.

Check that:
- plot_configs is a named list
- plot_metadata exists for each saved plot
- plot_code exists for each saved plot, if code is still stored
- plot names are valid, e.g. p1, p2, p3
- metadata plot names match config names

Repair where safe:
- remove metadata for plots that no longer exist
- add default metadata for plots missing metadata
- normalize sort_order
- default missing section_name to "Analysis"

4. Validate layout state.

Allowed layout_type values:
- Grid
- Sections

If missing or invalid:
- set layout_type = "Grid"
- add warning

layout_cols:
- coerce to integer
- clamp between 1 and 4
- default to 2 if invalid

5. Validate sections.

If section state exists:
- remove section references to plots that no longer exist
- drop empty sections
- rebuild sections from plot_metadata when possible

Important:
section layout should ultimately be derived from plot_metadata, not from a stale saved_sections object.

6. Validate data compatibility when data is available.

For each saved plot config:
- check required mapping columns exist in current data
- check XVar/YVar/ZVar/GroupVar/etc. columns exist if non-empty
- warn for missing optional columns
- error or mark plot as not rebuildable if required columns are missing

Do not crash the whole project because one plot cannot rebuild.

7. Rebuild plots defensively.

When loading a project:
- validate project state first
- restore state
- if data is available, attempt to rebuild each plot
- if a plot fails, keep its ... [truncated]

      saved_plots$code <- project_state$plot_code

            section_name = "Analysis",
            sort_order = next_sort_order(saved_plots$metadata)
          )
        }
      }

      saved_plots$plots <- list()
      project_data(NULL)
      project_data_info(list(

## 190. 2026-07-04T07:33:57.909Z - autoquant_origin - assistant

Added project validation and repair support in [app.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AutoPlots/app.R).

What changed:
- `validate_project_state(project_state, data = NULL)` now returns structured validation:
  - `valid`
  - `errors`
  - `warnings`
  - `repaired_state`
- It repairs safe stale project state:
  - missing/default metadata
  - stale metadata for deleted plots
  - mismatched metadata plot names
  - missing sections
  - invalid `layout_type`
  - invalid/clamped `layout_cols`
  - missing plot code
- Added data compatibility checks for saved plot mappings.
- Added per-plot statuses:
  - `Ready`
  - `Needs data`
  - `Missing columns`
  - `Rebuild failed`
- Saved plot table now shows `Status`.
- Project loading now:
  - validates and repairs first
  - restores configs/code/metadata
  - rebuilds plots defensively if data exists
  - keeps configs/metadata even when plots cannot rebuild
  - reports warnings and failed plots
- Report/layout/generated code now includes only `Ready` plots.

Verified:
- Validation helper smoke passed for normal, missing metadata, corrupt layout, missing version, and missing columns.
- Server smoke passed for:
  - normal load/rebuild
  - renamed source column -> `Missing columns`
  - missing source CSV -> `Needs data`
  - corrupt `layout_type` -> repaired to `Grid`
  - missing metadata -> default metadata added.


## 191. 2026-07-04T07:37:07.613Z - autoquant_origin - user

Add portable project bundle support to the Analytics Shiny App.

Goal:
Allow the user to save and load a self-contained local project folder containing the project state and a copy of the source data.

Rules:
- Do not modify AutoPlots.
- Do not call echarts4r directly.
- Do not implement cloud sync.
- Do not implement database storage.
- Do not implement zip export yet.
- Do not eval generated code from project files.
- Continue saving configs, not htmlwidget plot objects.
- Keep this local-first.
- Keep changes small and targeted.

Bundle format:
A project bundle is a folder:

<bundle_dir>/
  project.rds
  data.csv
  exports/

Requirements:

1. Add Project tab controls:
- textInput("bundle_dir", "Project Bundle Directory", value = file.path(getwd(), "autoplots_project"))
- actionButton("save_bundle", "Save Project Bundle", class = "btn-primary")
- actionButton("load_bundle", "Load Project Bundle", class = "btn-secondary")

2. Save Project Bundle.

On save_bundle:
- validate bundle_dir
- create bundle_dir if needed
- create file.path(bundle_dir, "exports") if needed
- save project state to file.path(bundle_dir, "project.rds")
- if uploaded/source data is available:
  - copy source CSV into file.path(bundle_dir, "data.csv")
  - update project state data_path to the bundled data.csv path
  - optionally store original_data_path separately
- saveRDS the updated bundled project state
- show success message with bundle path

3. Load Project Bundle.

On load_bundle:
- look for file.path(bundle_dir, "project.rds")
- load it using existing project validation/repair
- if file.path(bundle_dir, "data.csv") exists, prefer that data file over any absolute data_path stored inside project.rds
- reload data from bundled data.csv
- regenerate saved plots from configs
- restore layout/export settings
- set export_dir to file.path(bundle_dir, "exports") unless project state explicitly overrides it safely
- show success/warnings

4. Do not break existing Save Project / Load Project .rds behavior.

Keep both workflows:
- Save Project File
- Load Project File
- Save Project Bundle
- Load Project Bundle

5. Generated code behavior.

When loaded from a bundle, generated code should use the bundled data path, not the old absolute path.

Example:

data <- data.table::fread("C:/.../autoplots_project/data.csv")

6. Add validation.

Friendly errors for:
- missing bundle_dir
- missing project.rds
- missing bundled data.csv
- invalid project.rds
- failed data reload
- failed plot rebuild ... [truncated]

build_layout_code <- function(plot_names, section_plot_names = list(), layout_type = "Grid", cols = 2) {
  if (identical(layout_type, "Sections")) {
    return(paste0(
      "sections <- ", section_list_code(section_plot_names), "\n\n",
      "report <- AutoPlots::display_plots_sections(\n",
      "  sections = sections,\n",
      "  cols = ", cols, "\n",
      ")\n\n",
      "report"
    ))
  }

  paste0(
    "plots <- ", plot_list_code(plot_names), "\n\n",
    "report <- AutoPlots::display_plots_grid(\n",
    "  plots = plots,\n",
    "  cols = ", cols, "\n",
    ")\n\n",
    "report"
  )
}

build_export_code <- function(export_dir = "path/to/output", export_name = "autoplots_report") {
  paste0(
    "AutoPlots::save_widget(\n",
    "  widget = report,\n",
    "  path = ", r_string(export_dir), ",\n",
    "  name = ", r_string(export_name), ",\n",
    "  selfcontained = TRUE,\n",
    "  overwrite = TRUE,\n",
    "  open = FALSE\n",
    ")"
  )
}

build_report_code <- function(
  saved_code,
  section_plot_names = list(),
  layout_type = "Grid",
  cols = 2,
  export_dir = "path/to/output",
  export_name = "autoplots_report"
) {
  plot_names <- names(saved_code)
  assignment_code <- if (length(plot_names)) {
    paste(unlist(saved_code, use.names = FALSE), collapse = "\n\n")
  } else {
    ""
  }

  layout_code <- build_layout_code(
    plot_names = plot_names,
    section_plot_names = section_plot_names,
    layout_type = layout_type,
    cols = cols
  )

  paste0(
    "library(AutoPlots)\n\n",
    "data <- data.table::fread(\"path/to/data.csv\")\n\n",
    if (nzchar(assignment_code)) paste0(assignment_code, "\n\n") else "",
    layout_code,
    "\n\n",
    build_export_code(export_dir = export_dir, export_name = export_name)
  )
}

write_report_code <- function(code, path, name) {
  export_dir <- selected_value(path)
  if (is.null(export_dir)) {
    stop("Export directory is required.", call. = FALSE)
  }

  export_name <- selected_value(name)
  if (is.null(export_name)) {
    stop("File name is required.", call. = FALSE)
  }

  if (!dir.exists(export_dir)) {
    dir.create(export_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
  }

  if (!dir.exists(export_dir)) {
    stop("Export directory could not be created.", call. = FALSE)
  }

  stale_metadata <- setdiff(names(repaired$plot_metadata), plot_names)
  if (length(stale_metadata)) {
    repaired$plot_metadata[stale_metadata] <- NULL
    result$warnings <- c(
      result$warnings,
      paste("Removed stale metadata for missing plots:", paste(stale_metadata, collapse = ", "))
    )
  }

  for (plot_name in plot_names) {
    config <- repaired$plot_configs[[plot_name]]

    if (is.null(repaired$plot_code[[plot_name]])) {
      repaired$plot_code[[plot_name]] <- build_autoplots_assignment_code(plot_name, config)
      result$warnings <- c(result$warnings, paste("Rebuilt missing code for", plot_name))
    }

    metadata <- repaired$plot_metadata[[plot_name]]
    if (is.null(metadata) || !is.list(metadata)) {
      metadata <- plot_metadata(
        plot_name = plot_name,
        config = config,
        section_name = "Analysis",
        sort_order = NA_integer_
      )
      result$warnings <- c(result$warnings, paste("Added default metadata for", plot_name))
    }

    if (!identical(metadata$plot_name, plot_name)) {
      metadata$plot_name <- plot_name
      result$warnings <- c(result$warnings, paste("Repaired metadata plot name for", plot_name))
    }
    metadata$plot_type <- config$plot_type
    if (is.null(metadata$section_name) || !nzchar(metadata$section_name)) {
      metadata$section_name <- "Analysis"
      result$warnings <- c(result$warnings, paste("Defaulted section to Analysis for", plot_name))
    }
    metadata$sort_order <- suppressWarnings(as.integer(metadata$sort_order))
    repaired$plot_metadata[[plot_name]] <- metadata
  }

  ordered_names <- ordered_plot_names_from_metadata(repaired$plot_metadata)
  for (index in seq_along(ordered_names)) {
    repaired$plot_metadata[[ordered_names[index]]]$sort_order <- index
  }

  if (is.null(repaired$layout_type) || !repaired$layout_type %in% c("Grid", "Sections")) {
    repaired$layout_type <- "Grid"
    result$warnings <- c(result$warnings, "Invalid layout_type repaired to Grid.")
  }

  layout_cols <- suppressWarnings(as.integer(repaired$layout_cols))
  if (is.na(layout_cols)) {
    layout_cols <- 2L
    result$warnings <- c(result$warnings, "Invalid layout_cols repaired to 2.")
  }
  repaired$layout_cols <- max(1L, min(4L, layout_cols))

server <- function(input, output, session) {
  mapping_state <- reactiveValues(values = list())
  saved_plots <- reactiveValues(
    plots = list(),
    configs = list(),
    code = list(),
    metadata = list(),
    status = list()
  )
  saved_sections <- reactiveValues(
    sections = list()
  )
  plot_result <- reactiveVal(NULL)
  plot_error <- reactiveVal(NULL)
  plot_config <- reactiveVal(NULL)
  plot_list_message <- reactiveVal("")
  export_message <- reactiveVal("")
  project_message <- reactiveVal("")
  project_data <- reactiveVal(NULL)
  project_data_info <- reactiveVal(list(path = NULL, name = NULL))

  layout_cols_value <- function() {
    cols <- input$layout_cols
    if (is.null(cols) || is.na(cols)) {
      return(2)
    }

    as.integer(cols)
  }

  export_name_value <- function() {
    name <- selected_value(input$export_name)
    if (is.null(name)) {
      return(NULL)
    }

    tools::file_path_sans_ext(basename(name))
  }

  export_output_path <- function(export_dir, export_name) {
    file.path(export_dir, paste0(export_name, ".html"))
  }

  current_report_code <- function() {
    ready_names <- ready_plot_names()
    if (!length(ready_names)) {
      stop("No ready saved plots are available. Rebuild or repair saved plots before exporting R code.", call. = FALSE)
    }

    build_report_code(
      saved_code = ordered_list_by_names(saved_plots$code, ready_names),
      section_plot_names = section_plot_names(ready_only = TRUE),
      layout_type = input$layout_type,
      cols = layout_cols_value(),
      export_dir = default_value(selected_value(input$export_dir), "path/to/output"),
      export_name = default_value(export_name_value(), "autoplots_report")
    )
  }

  ordered_plot_names <- function() {
    ordered_plot_names_from_metadata(saved_plots$metadata)
  }

  ready_plot_names <- function() {
    plot_names <- ordered_plot_names()
    plot_names[vapply(plot_names, function(plot_name) {
      identical(saved_plots$status[[plot_name]]$status, "Ready")
    }, logical(1))]
  }

  ordered_saved_plots <- function() {
    ordered_list_by_names(saved_plots$plots, ready_plot_names())
  }

  section_plot_names <- function(ready_only = FALSE) {
    metadata <- saved_plots$metadata
    if (isTRUE(ready_only)) {
      metadata <- ordered_list_by_names(metadata, ready_plot_names())
    }

    section_plot_names_from_metadata(metadata)
  }

  section_plot_object ... [truncated]

## 202. 2026-07-04T15:43:37.459Z - autoquant_origin - user

## Electron wrapper repository

This app may be run inside an Electron shell maintained outside the Analytics Shiny App repository.

Known Electron wrapper source:
- AdrianAntico/<ELECTRON_FORK_REPO_NAME>

The Analytics Shiny App repository owns:
- Shiny app logic
- AutoPlots calls
- project state
- export behavior
- generated report code
- UI behavior inside the Shiny app

The Electron wrapper repository owns:
- Electron startup
- R/Shiny process launch
- local server lifecycle
- app window behavior
- packaged desktop behavior
- installer/build configuration if present
- filesystem permissions or path bridging specific to Electron

When an Electron smoke test fails, first determine whether the failure belongs to the Shiny app repo or the Electron wrapper repo.

Likely Shiny app repo issues:
- plot build fails in browser and Electron
- project save/load fails in browser and Electron
- export function fails in browser and Electron
- generated code is wrong
- UI observer/reactive logic fails

Likely Electron wrapper repo issues:
- Electron app does not launch
- R/Shiny backend does not start
- browser version works but Electron version does not
- file paths behave differently only in Electron
- export permissions differ only in Electron
- app closes but R process remains running
- packaged app cannot find R/packages/app files
- environment variables/API keys are not available in Electron

If the issue appears Electron-specific, inspect and modify the Electron wrapper repository, not the Analytics Shiny App repo.


## 203. 2026-07-04T15:43:44.364Z - autoquant_origin - assistant

Got it. I’ll treat Electron as a separate wrapper boundary going forward:

- Analytics Shiny App owns Shiny behavior, AutoPlots calls, project state, exports, generated code, and in-app UI logic.
- Electron wrapper owns process launch, local server lifecycle, window behavior, packaging/installers, Electron-only file/path behavior, and desktop runtime concerns.

For future Electron smoke failures, I’ll first classify whether the same issue reproduces in the normal browser Shiny app. If it’s browser + Electron, I’ll work in the Shiny app repo. If it’s Electron-only, I’ll inspect the Electron wrapper repo instead.

Codex previously worked on Adrian's forked repository for electronifying Shiny apps. The Analytics Shiny App may depend on that external Electron wrapper repo for desktop packaging/runtime behavior.

Add a section named "Electron wrapper repository".

Include:
- the wrapper repo is separate from the Analytics Shiny App repo
- the app repo owns Shiny logic, AutoPlots calls, project state, exports, generated code, and UI behavior
- the Electron wrapper repo owns Electron startup, R/Shiny process launch, desktop window behavior, packaging, local server lifecycle, and Electron-specific file/path behavior
- when Electron smoke tests fail, classify the issue as app-level or wrapper-level before changing code
- if the failure happens in both browser Shiny and Electron, fix the Analytics Shiny App repo
- if the failure happens only in Electron, inspect/fix the Electron wrapper repo
- include likely examples of app-level failures and wrapper-level failures

## 207. 2026-07-04T15:46:13.763Z - autoquant_origin - user

Refactor export logic into a standard service-result pattern.

Goal:
Start applying the service contract architecture by moving HTML/R-code/Export All logic into a bounded export service.

Rules:
- Do not modify AutoPlots.
- Do not change user-facing behavior.
- Do not redesign the UI.
- Do not refactor project, plot, or GenAI services yet.
- Keep changes small and targeted.
- Preserve existing export smoke tests.

Create if appropriate:
R/services/service_result.R
R/services/export_service.R

If the app is not structured as an R package, place these helpers wherever current app helper functions live, but keep them clearly separated.

Requirements:

1. Add a standard service result helper.

Create a helper like:

service_result <- function(
  status = c("success", "warning", "error", "needs_input"),
  value = NULL,
  artifacts = list(),
  messages = character(),
  warnings = character(),
  errors = character(),
  diagnostics = list(),
  code = NULL,
  metadata = list()
)

It should return a named list.

2. Add export service helpers.

Implement helpers such as:

validate_export_config(export_dir, export_name)

export_html_service(report, export_dir, export_name)

export_code_service(code, export_dir, export_name)

export_all_service(report, code, export_dir, export_name)

3. Validation should handle:
- missing report
- missing code
- missing export_dir
- missing export_name
- invalid filename characters if relevant
- directory does not exist
- directory creation failure
- write permission failure
- AutoPlots::save_widget failure
- writeLines failure

4. Export services should never throw raw errors to Shiny.

Use tryCatch and return service_result(status = "error", errors = ...).

5. On success, return artifacts with paths.

Example:

artifacts = list(
  html_path = "C:/.../report.html",
  code_path = "C:/.../report.R"
)

6. Update Shiny observers for:
- Export HTML
- Export R Code
- Export All

They should:
- call the service
- display messages/errors from the returned service_result
- avoid duplicating validation logic in observers

7. Preserve generated report code behavior.

Do not change the generated code output except where currently required for export paths.

8. Add simple smoke/test helper if practical.

Example:

.ap_qa_export_service <- function()

or a documented manual test section.

9. Smoke tests:
- Export HTML still works.

build_export_code <- function(export_dir = "path/to/output", export_name = "autoplots_report") {
  paste0(
    "AutoPlots::save_widget(\n",
    "  widget = report,\n",
    "  path = ", r_string(export_dir), ",\n",
    "  name = ", r_string(export_name), ",\n",
    "  selfcontained = TRUE,\n",
    "  overwrite = TRUE,\n",
    "  open = FALSE\n",
    ")"
  )
}

build_report_code <- function(
  saved_code,
  section_plot_names = list(),
  layout_type = "Grid",
  cols = 2,
  export_dir = "path/to/output",
  export_name = "autoplots_report",
  data_path = "path/to/data.csv"
) {
  plot_names <- names(saved_code)
  assignment_code <- if (length(plot_names)) {
    paste(unlist(saved_code, use.names = FALSE), collapse = "\n\n")
  } else {
    ""
  }

  layout_code <- build_layout_code(
    plot_names = plot_names,
    section_plot_names = section_plot_names,
    layout_type = layout_type,
    cols = cols
  )

  paste0(
    "library(AutoPlots)\n\n",
    "data <- data.table::fread(", r_string(data_path), ")\n\n",
    if (nzchar(assignment_code)) paste0(assignment_code, "\n\n") else "",
    layout_code,
    "\n\n",
    build_export_code(export_dir = export_dir, export_name = export_name)
  )
}

write_report_code <- function(code, path, name) {
  export_dir <- selected_value(path)
  if (is.null(export_dir)) {
    stop("Export directory is required.", call. = FALSE)
  }

  export_name <- selected_value(name)
  if (is.null(export_name)) {
    stop("File name is required.", call. = FALSE)
  }

  if (!dir.exists(export_dir)) {
    dir.create(export_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
  }

  if (!dir.exists(export_dir)) {
    stop("Export directory could not be created.", call. = FALSE)
  }

  output_path <- file.path(
    normalizePath(export_dir, winslash = "/", mustWork = TRUE),
    paste0(tools::file_path_sans_ext(basename(export_name)), ".R")
  )

  writeLines(code, con = output_path, useBytes = TRUE)
  normalizePath(output_path, winslash = "/", mustWork = TRUE)
}

normalize_project_path <- function(path) {
  path <- selected_value(path)
  if (is.null(path)) {
    stop("Project file path is required.", call. = FALSE)
  }

  if (!grepl("\\.rds$", path, ignore.case = TRUE)) {
    path <- paste0(path, ".rds")
  }

  path
}

## 212. 2026-07-04T15:50:36.273Z - autoquant_origin - user

Extract the Analytics Shiny App from the AutoPlots repo into a separate app repo.

Goal:
Separate the app/product code from the AutoPlots plotting package.

Rules:
- Do not rewrite the app.
- Do not modify AutoPlots plotting functions.
- Do not move AutoPlots internals into the app repo.
- Copy first; do not delete from AutoPlots until the app runs from the new repo.
- The new app repo should depend on AutoPlots as an external package.
- Keep changes small and reversible.

New repo:
<NEW_REPO_NAME>

Migration plan:

1. Identify app-specific files in the AutoPlots repo:
- app.R or main Shiny entry point
- app-specific R helpers
- registries
- services
- project/export helpers
- www assets
- docs/product_scope.md
- docs/service_contract.md
- docs/electron_smoke_test.md
- docs/product_roadmap.md
- sample data if present

2. Copy app-specific files into the new repo.

Suggested structure:
- app.R
- R/services/
- R/registries/
- R/project/
- R/utils/
- www/
- docs/
- inst/sample_data/
- README.md

3. Add a DESCRIPTION or renv/setup file for app dependencies.

Include AutoPlots as an external dependency.

4. Remove any source() calls or relative references that assume the app lives inside AutoPlots.

The app should load AutoPlots with:

library(AutoPlots)

5. Update paths.

Ensure project save/load, bundles, exports, and sample data use paths relative to the app repo or user-selected paths, not the old AutoPlots repo.

6. Add README.md.

Include:
- what the app is
- dependency on AutoPlots
- how to run locally
- how to run Electron smoke test if applicable
- link/reference to Electron wrapper repo
- basic smoke test checklist

7. Run smoke tests:
- app launches from new repo
- upload CSV
- build Line
- add p1
- build Bar
- add p2
- grid preview
- section preview
- export HTML
- export R code
- save/load project
- save/load bundle

8. After the new repo passes, leave a note in AutoPlots docs pointing to the app repo.

Do not delete the old app copy from AutoPlots until explicitly requested.

# Electron Smoke Test Notes

## Electron wrapper repository

The Electron wrapper repository is separate from the Analytics Shiny App repository.

The Analytics Shiny App repository owns:

- Shiny app logic
- AutoPlots calls
- project state
- export behavior
- generated report code
- UI behavior inside the Shiny app

The Electron wrapper repository owns:

- Electron startup
- R/Shiny process launch
- desktop window behavior
- packaging
- local server lifecycle
- Electron-specific file/path behavior

When Electron smoke tests fail, first classify the issue as app-level or wrapper-level before changing code.

If the failure happens in both browser Shiny and Electron, fix the Analytics Shiny App repository.

Likely app-level failures:

- plot build fails in browser and Electron
- project save/load fails in browser and Electron
- export function fails in browser and Electron
- generated report code is wrong
- Shiny UI observer or reactive logic fails in both environments
- AutoPlots arguments are generated incorrectly

If the failure happens only in Electron, inspect and fix the Electron wrapper repository.

Likely wrapper-level failures:

- Electron app does not launch
- R/Shiny backend does not start
- browser Shiny works but Electron does not
- file paths behave differently only in Electron
- export permissions differ only in Electron
- app closes but the R process remains running
- packaged app cannot find R, packages, or app files
- environment variables or API keys are unavailable only in Electron

This boundary keeps Shiny app behavior and desktop runtime behavior from being tangled together during debugging.

Stabilize the extracted Analytics Shiny App repo after moving it out of AutoPlots.

Goal:
Make sure the app repo is clean, package-like, runnable, and no longer depends on being inside the AutoPlots repo.

Rules:
- Do not add new product features.
- Do not modify AutoPlots plotting functions.
- Do not create subdirectories inside R/.
- Keep R/ flat.
- Keep changes small and targeted.

1. Confirm app structure.

app.R
DESCRIPTION
README.md

## 238. 2026-07-04T16:22:22.586Z - analytics_shinyapp_continuation - tool_output

Output:
# Analytics Shiny App

Analytics Shiny App is a local-first Shiny visualization builder powered by AutoPlots.

It lets users load data, build AutoPlots charts, save plots, organize report layouts, export HTML/R code, and save or load local project bundles.

## Repository Boundary

This repository owns the app/product layer:

- Shiny app logic
- AutoPlots calls
- plot registries and options
- project state
- export behavior
- generated report code
- UI behavior inside the Shiny app

The AutoPlots package remains an external dependency. AutoPlots plotting internals should not be copied into this repository.

## Dependencies

Required R packages include:

- `AutoPlots`
- `shiny`
- `data.table`
- `echarts4r`
- `htmltools`
- `tools`

Install or load AutoPlots separately before running this app.

## Run Locally

From this repository root:

```r
shiny::runApp(".")
```

Or from a terminal:

```powershell
Rscript -e "shiny::runApp('.')"
```

## Electron Wrapper

This app may be run inside an Electron shell maintained outside this repository.

Known Electron wrapper source:

- `AdrianAntico/<ELECTRON_FORK_REPO_NAME>`

The Electron wrapper repository owns Electron startup, R/Shiny process launch, desktop window behavior, packaging, local server lifecycle, and Electron-specific file/path behavior.

If a smoke test fails in both browser Shiny and Electron, fix this repository. If it fails only in Electron, inspect the Electron wrapper repository first.

See `docs/electron_smoke_test.md`.

## Smoke Test Checklist

Basic Shiny app smoke:

- app launches from this repository
- upload CSV
- build Line plot
- add `p1`
- build Bar plot
- add `p2`
- preview Grid layout
- assign sections
- preview Sections layout
- export R code
- export HTML when `AutoPlots::save_widget()` is available
- save/load project `.rds`
- save/load project bundle

Generated code smoke:

- report code uses `AutoPlots::<PlotType>()`
- report code uses `AutoPlots::display_plots_grid()` or `AutoPlots::display_plots_sections()`
- bundle-loaded report code uses the bundled `data.csv` path

## Notes

This repository was copied out of the AutoPlots package repository so app/product code can evolve separately from the plotting package. Do not delete the old app copy from AutoPlots until this repository has passed the expected app and Electron smoke tests.

build_plot_args <- function(
  plot_type,
  data,
  input,
  include_data = TRUE
) {
  args <- list()
  if (include_data) {
    args$dt <- data
  }

  for (mapping in active_mappings(plot_type)) {
    args[[mapping]] <- mapping_value(input, mapping)
  }

  for (option_name in plot_spec(plot_type)$options) {
    args <- add_option_arg(args, option_name, option_value(input, option_name))
  }

  args[!vapply(args, is.null, logical(1))]
}

build_autoplots_call <- function(plot_type, data, input) {
  plot_fun <- getExportedValue("AutoPlots", plot_type)
  args <- build_plot_args(plot_type, data, input)
  do.call(plot_fun, args)
}

snapshot_plot_config <- function(plot_type, input, mapping_values = list()) {
  spec <- plot_spec(plot_type)

  mappings <- list()
  for (mapping in active_mappings(plot_type)) {
    value <- mapping_value(input, mapping)
    if (is_empty_arg(value)) {
      remembered_value <- mapping_values[[mapping]]
      if (!is_empty_arg(remembered_value)) {
        value <- remembered_value
      }
    }

    mappings[[mapping]] <- value
  }

  options <- list()
  for (option_name in spec$options) {
    options[[option_name]] <- option_value(input, option_name)
  }

  list(
    plot_type = plot_type,
    mappings = mappings,
    options = options
  )
}

build_plot_args_from_config <- function(config, data, include_data = TRUE) {
  args <- list()

  if (include_data) {
    args$dt <- data
  }

  for (name in names(config$mappings)) {
    args[[name]] <- config$mappings[[name]]
  }

  for (name in names(config$options)) {
    args <- add_option_arg(args, name, config$options[[name]])
  }

  args[!vapply(args, is_empty_arg, logical(1))]
}

build_autoplots_call_from_config <- function(config, data) {
  plot_fun <- getExportedValue("AutoPlots", config$plot_type)
  args <- build_plot_args_from_config(config, data = data, include_data = TRUE)
  do.call(plot_fun, args)
}

validate_plot_ready <- function(plot_type, data, input) {
  if (is.null(data)) {
    return("No data is available.")
  }

  spec <- plot_spec(plot_type)

  for (mapping in spec$mappings) {
    value <- mapping_value(input, mapping)

    if (is.null(value) || length(value) == 0L || any(value == "")) {
      return(paste0("Required mapping is missing: ", mapping))
    }

    if (identical(mapping, "CorrVars")) {
      missing_cols <- setdiff(value, names(data))
      if (length(missing_cols)) {
        return(paste0(
          "Cor ... [truncated]

mapping_control <- function(mapping, data, required = TRUE, selected = NULL) {
  choices <- column_choices(data, include_none = !required)
  if (identical(mapping, "CorrVars")) {
    all_choices <- column_choices(data)
    if (is.null(selected)) {
      selected <- names(data)
    }

    selected <- selected[selected %in% all_choices]
    if (!length(selected)) {
      selected <- all_choices
    }

    return(selectInput(
      mapping_input_id(mapping),
      mapping,
      choices = all_choices,
      selected = selected,
      multiple = TRUE
    ))
  }

  if (is.null(selected) || !selected %in% choices) {
    if (!required && "" %in% choices) {
      selected <- ""
    } else {
      selected <- choices[1L]
    }
  }

  selectInput(
    mapping_input_id(mapping),
    mapping,
    choices = choices,
    selected = selected
  )
}

  output$preview_plot <- renderUI({
    if (is.null(input$build_plot) || input$build_plot == 0L) {
      return(tags$div(
        style = "padding: 16px; color: #6B7280;",
        "Configure the plot options, then click Build / Refresh Plot."
      ))
    }

    if (is.null(input$csv_file) && is.null(project_data())) {
      return(plot_error_message("No data is available."))
    }

    if (!is.null(input$csv_file$size) &&
        input$csv_file$size > MAX_UPLOAD_MB * 1024^2) {
      return(plot_error_message(sprintf(
        "Uploaded file is too large. Limit is %s MB.",
        MAX_UPLOAD_MB
      )))
    }

    if (!is.null(plot_error())) {
      return(plot_error_message(plot_error()))
    }

    if (is.null(plot_result())) {
      return(tags$div(
        style = "padding: 16px; color: #6B7280;",
        "Preparing plot preview..."
      ))
    }

    echarts4r::echarts4rOutput("preview_plot_widget", height = "600px")
  })

  output$preview_plot_widget <- echarts4r::renderEcharts4r({
    req(plot_result())
    plot_result()
  })

  output$generated_code <- renderText({
    req(input$plot_type)

    build_autoplots_code(
      plot_type = input$plot_type,
      input = input
    )
  })

  output$plot_list_message <- renderText({
    plot_list_message()
  })

## 262. 2026-07-04T16:35:21.145Z - analytics_shinyapp_continuation - assistant

There’s no exported AutoPlots HTML-save helper in the installed package, so a direct `AutoPlots::save_widget()` keeps the extracted app brittle. I’m adding a tiny app-side export fallback through `htmlwidgets::saveWidget()` while still preferring `AutoPlots::save_widget()` when a future/local AutoPlots build exports it. Generated report code will mirror that fallback instead of calling a non-exported function.

build_export_code <- function(export_dir = "path/to/output", export_name = "autoplots_report") {
  paste0(
    "AutoPlots::save_widget(\n",
    "  widget = report,\n",
    "  path = ", r_string(export_dir), ",\n",
    "  name = ", r_string(export_name), ",\n",
    "  selfcontained = TRUE,\n",
    "  overwrite = TRUE,\n",
    "  open = FALSE\n",
    ")"
  )
}

build_report_code <- function(
  saved_code,
  section_plot_names = list(),
  layout_type = "Grid",
  cols = 2,
  export_dir = "path/to/output",
  export_name = "autoplots_report",
  data_path = "path/to/data.csv"
) {
  plot_names <- names(saved_code)
  assignment_code <- if (length(plot_names)) {
    paste(unlist(saved_code, use.names = FALSE), collapse = "\n\n")
  } else {
    ""
  }

  layout_code <- build_layout_code(
    plot_names = plot_names,
    section_plot_names = section_plot_names,

## 266. 2026-07-04T16:36:38.222Z - analytics_shinyapp_continuation - assistant

The grid report is an `htmltools` tag containing widgets, not a single `htmlwidget`; that explains the fallback failure. I’m testing `htmltools::save_html()` now, which is appropriate for tag-based reports. If AutoPlots later exports `save_widget`, the app will still prefer it for self-contained output.


## 267. 2026-07-04T16:36:57.610Z - analytics_shinyapp_continuation - assistant

`htmltools::save_html()` works for the tag-based report. I’ll make that the final fallback and include a warning in the service result that this fallback writes an asset directory when `AutoPlots::save_widget()` is unavailable, so the app is honest instead of silently claiming self-contained output.

## 271. 2026-07-04T16:38:50.819Z - analytics_shinyapp_continuation - assistant

Stabilization pass is complete.

I split the monolithic app into the flat package-like structure under [R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AnalyticsShinyApp/R), with no `R/services`, `R/registries`, `R/project`, or `R/utils` subdirectories. `app.R` is now a bootstrap that checks dependencies, loads `library(AutoPlots)`, sources the flat modules, builds UI/server, and launches the app.

Key changes:
- Added flat modules: `app_ui.R`, `app_server.R`, `registry_plots.R`, `registry_options.R`, `service_project.R`, `service_plot.R`, `project_state.R`, `project_bundle.R`, `utils_paths.R`, `utils_messages.R`.
- Added `check_app_dependencies()` in [utils_paths.R](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AnalyticsShinyApp/R/utils_paths.R).
- Removed direct `echarts4r` app usage and dependency.
- Added sample CSV: [app_qa_transactional.csv](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AnalyticsShinyApp/inst/sample_data/app_qa_transactional.csv).
- Added [docs/smoke_test.md](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AnalyticsShinyApp/docs/smoke_test.md).
- Updated [README.md](C:/Users/Bizon/Documents/GitHub/AnalyticsShinyApp/README.md) with dependencies, local run steps, local AutoPlots dev install guidance, smoke tests, and Electron repo boundary.
- HTML export now prefers exported `AutoPlots::save_widget()` when available. This installed AutoPlots does not export it, so the app falls back to `htmltools::save_html()` and returns a warning that an asset directory is written beside the HTML.

Verified:
- `source('app.R', local = new.env())` passes.
- `shiny::runApp('.')` launches from this repo and reaches `Listening on http://127.0.0.1:39221`.
- Sample data builds Line and Bar plots through AutoPlots package calls.
- Grid and section reports build through `AutoPlots::display_plots_grid()` / `display_plots_sections()`.
- R code export, HTML export fallback, project save, and bundle directory path checks pass.
- Production `app.R`/`R/` search is clean for old AutoPlots repo assumptions, direct `echarts4r`, `devtools::load_all`, internal AutoPlots file sourcing, and forbidden nested `R/` paths.

Git still shows everything as untracked, which matches the extracted repo state before this pass.


## 272. 2026-07-04T16:41:34.901Z - analytics_shinyapp_continuation - user

Am I good to remove the files from AutoPlots?


## 273. 2026-07-04T16:41:46.873Z - analytics_shinyapp_continuation - assistant

Almost, but I would not delete them from AutoPlots until you do two guardrail steps.

You’re good to remove the old **app/product files** from AutoPlots after:

1. Commit or push this extracted `AnalyticsShinyApp` repo first.  
   Right now this repo’s files are still untracked, so deletion from AutoPlots would remove your fallback before the new repo is safely recorded.

2. Run the AutoPlots package checks after removing the old app files.  
   Make sure you only remove the old Shiny app/product layer, not exported plotting functions or anything AutoPlots still needs.

Safe removal targets are likely things like the old copied `app.R`, app docs, Shiny-only project/export UI files, and old sample app assets inside AutoPlots.

Do not remove:
- AutoPlots exported plotting functions
- `display_plots_grid()`
- `display_plots_sections()`
- any package exports used by generated app code
- anything needed by AutoPlots tests/docs/examples unless you update those references

My recommendation: first make an initial commit in `AnalyticsShinyApp`, then in AutoPlots remove the old app copy in a separate small commit titled something like `Remove extracted Shiny app files`. Then run AutoPlots package checks.

mapping_control <- function(mapping, data, required = TRUE, selected = NULL) {
  choices <- column_choices(data, include_none = !required)
  if (identical(mapping, "CorrVars")) {
    all_choices <- column_choices(data)
    if (is.null(selected)) {
      selected <- names(data)
    }

    selected <- selected[selected %in% all_choices]
    if (!length(selected)) {
      selected <- all_choices
    }

    return(selectInput(
      mapping_input_id(mapping),
      mapping,
      choices = all_choices,
      selected = selected,
      multiple = TRUE
    ))
  }

  if (is.null(selected) || !selected %in% choices) {
    if (!required && "" %in% choices) {
      selected <- ""
    } else {
      selected <- choices[1L]
    }
  }

  selectInput(
    mapping_input_id(mapping),
    mapping,
    choices = choices,
    selected = selected
  )
}

            actionButton("export_all", "Export All", class = "btn-success")
          )
        ),
        ui_card(
          title = "Export Status",
          class = "aq-export-status",
          uiOutput("export_message")
        )
      )
    )
  )
  )
}

## 287. 2026-07-04T17:19:28.317Z - analytics_shinyapp_continuation - user

Prepare the Analytics Shiny App CSS for multiple app themes.

Goal:
Introduce a CSS-variable-based theme foundation so the app can later support Light, Dark, and Pimp modes.

Rules:
- Do not add new UI dependencies.
- Do not add the full settings UI yet unless trivial.
- Do not change app behavior.
- Do not redesign all tabs.
- Do not modify AutoPlots.
- Keep changes small and targeted.

Tasks:

1. Update www/app.css to use app-level CSS variables.

Define default variables for:
- --aq-bg
- --aq-surface
- --aq-surface-2
- --aq-border
- --aq-text
- --aq-muted
- --aq-primary
- --aq-primary-hover
- --aq-success
- --aq-warning
- --aq-danger
- --aq-radius
- --aq-shadow
- --aq-input-bg
- --aq-input-border
- --aq-nav-active-bg
- --aq-nav-active-text

2. Update existing UI classes to use variables.

Classes likely include:
- aq-page
- aq-card
- aq-section-header
- aq-empty-state
- aq-status-badge
- aq-action-row
- aq-control-group
- aq-preview-panel
- aq-code-panel

3. Add three theme classes.

body.aq-theme-light
body.aq-theme-dark
body.aq-theme-pimp

Light should be the current/default clean style.

Dark should use:
- dark navy/charcoal background
- dark cards
- readable light text
- subtle borders

Pimp should use:
- dark or deep gradient background
- neon accent variables
- slightly stronger card borders/shadows
- but keep input text readable

4. Add a temporary simple way to apply a theme for testing.

Acceptable options:
- hardcode body class in UI for now
- or add a simple selectInput only if easy

Preferred minimal implementation:
- add helper ui_app_shell(..., theme = "light")
- applies class paste0("aq-theme-", theme)

Do not build full persistent app settings yet.

5. Ensure existing Export tab still looks good in light mode.

6. Quick manual theme test:
- apply aq-theme-light
- apply aq-theme-dark
- apply aq-theme-pimp
- confirm Export tab remains readable
- confirm inputs remain readable
- confirm buttons remain visually distinct
- confirm empty state remains readable

## 289. 2026-07-04T17:37:15.989Z - analytics_shinyapp_continuation - user

Update the report artifact UX design to include separation of duties between Artifact Generator modules and Display pages.

Goal:
Clarify that analytical modules create artifacts, while display/report pages select, arrange, and render artifacts.

Rules:
- Documentation only.
- Do not change app code.
- Do not modify AutoPlots.
- Preserve the current display_plots_grid() and display_plots_sections() direction.

Add a section: "Separation of Duties"

Include:

1. Artifact Generator modules
Examples:
- Plot Builder
- EDA
- Forecasting
- Modeling
- Target Analysis
- GenAI Narrative

Responsibilities:
- collect module-specific configuration
- validate inputs
- run analysis/modeling/forecasting
- create artifacts
- preview artifacts internally for tuning/customization
- return standard artifact objects

Non-responsibilities:
- final report layout
- global section ordering
- export orchestration
- project-level display decisions

2. Display / Report pages

Responsibilities:
- show artifact library
- select artifacts for report
- assign sections
- set ordering
- configure display mode
- render combined output
- export combined output

Non-responsibilities:
- running forecasting/modeling/EDA logic
- owning module-specific configuration
- modifying raw analytical results

3. Artifact Library

Define it as the bridge between generators and display.

It should track:
- artifact_id
- artifact_type
- source_module
- label
- section
- order
- visible
- status
- config
- code
- metadata

4. Forecasting example

Describe a Forecasting module that generates:
- forecast plot
- actual vs fitted plot
- residual plot
- backtest metrics table
- forecast values table
- forecast summary text
- methodology/caveat text

Explain that these artifacts can be previewed inside the Forecasting module but are selected/arranged in the Display page.

5. Display function direction

Keep:
- display_plots_grid()
- display_plots_sections()

Potentially add:
- display_plots_carousel()

For mixed artifacts, propose:
- display_report_grid()
- display_report_sections()
- display_report_carousel()
- display_report_canvas()

6. Core rule

Generator modules may preview artifacts internally, but final report composition belongs to the Display layer.


## 290. 2026-07-04T17:38:53.464Z - analytics_shinyapp_continuation - user

Implement the first artifact model foundation for the Analytics Shiny App.

Goal:
Create a generic report artifact structure so the app can eventually manage plots, text, tables, metrics, forecasts, and GenAI narratives through one consistent system.

Rules:
- Do not add new product features yet.
- Do not modify AutoPlots.
- Do not rewrite saved plots completely yet.
- Do not break existing saved plot behavior.
- Keep R/ flat. Do not create subdirectories under R/.
- Keep changes small and targeted.
- This is a foundation pass, not a full migration.

Create:
R/artifact_model.R

Implement:

1. create_artifact()

Function signature:

create_artifact <- function(
  artifact_id,
  artifact_type,
  label,
  source_module,
  object = NULL,
  content = NULL,
  config = list(),
  code = NULL,
  metadata = list(),
  section = "Analysis",
  order = NA_integer_,
  visible = TRUE,
  status = "ready",
  created_at = Sys.time(),
  updated_at = Sys.time()
)

Return a named list with class c("aq_artifact", "list").

2. validate_artifact()

Validate:
- artifact_id is non-empty character
- artifact_type is one of:
  - plot
  - table
  - text
  - metric
  - section_header
  - model_summary
  - forecast_block
  - genai_narrative
- label is non-empty
- source_module is non-empty
- status is one of:
  - ready
  - draft
  - needs_data
  - missing_columns
  - rebuild_failed
  - hidden
- section is non-empty
- visible is TRUE/FALSE

Return a service_result object.

3. artifact_summary()

Return a small data.table/data.frame with:
- artifact_id
- artifact_type
- label
- source_module
- section
- order
- visible
- status

4. as_plot_artifact()

Adapter helper for current saved plot objects.

Function signature:

as_plot_artifact <- function(
  plot_name,
  plot_object,
  config,
  code,
  metadata = list()
)

It should return create_artifact(
  artifact_id = plot_name,
  artifact_type = "plot",
  label = best available title or plot_name,
  source_module = "plot_builder",
  object = plot_object,
  config = config,
  code = code,
  metadata = metadata,
  section = metadata$section_name %||% "Analysis",
  order = metadata$sort_order %||% NA_integer_,
  status = metadata$status %||% "ready"
)

If `%||%` helper does not exist, add a small local helper.

5. Do not migrate all app state yet.

The app may continue using saved_plots for now.

But add a helper:

saved_plots_to_artifacts(saved_plots, configs, code, metadata)

This should return a named list of plot artifacts.

6. Add document ... [truncated]

library(AutoPlots)

app_env$MAX_UPLOAD_MB <- 50
options(shiny.maxRequestSize = app_env$MAX_UPLOAD_MB * 1024^2)

source(file.path("R", "registry_plots.R"), local = app_env)

server <- app_env$server

shinyApp(ui, server)

Add manual text artifact support to the Analytics Shiny App.

Goal:
Prove the new artifact model by allowing users to create, preview, organize, and include text artifacts in report output.

Rules:
- Do not add GenAI yet.
- Do not modify AutoPlots.
- Do not rewrite the whole saved plot system.
- Do not add table artifacts yet.
- Keep R/ flat. Do not create subdirectories under R/.
- Preserve existing saved plot behavior.
- Keep changes small and targeted.

Add Text Block

- text_artifact_label
- text_artifact_section
- text_artifact_content

Optional if easy:
- text_artifact_subtype with choices:
  - markdown
  - note
  - summary
  - caveat
  - methodology

Actions:
- Add Text Artifact
- Preview Text Artifact

2. Create text artifact on click.

Use create_artifact():

create_artifact(
  artifact_id = next artifact id, e.g. t1, t2, t3,
  artifact_type = "text",
  label = input$text_artifact_label,
  source_module = "manual_text",
  content = input$text_artifact_content,
  config = list(format = "markdown"),
  metadata = list(subtype = input$text_artifact_subtype),
  section = input$text_artifact_section,
  order = next order,
  status = "ready"
)

3. Store text artifacts separately for now.

Do not fully migrate saved_plots yet.

Acceptable temporary state:

saved_text_artifacts <- reactiveValues(
  artifacts = list()
)

or, if the app already has artifact state from artifact_model.R, use that.

4. Add text artifacts to artifact summary.

Create a combined artifact summary helper if useful:

combined_artifact_summary <- function(plot_artifacts, text_artifacts)

It should show:
- artifact_id
- artifact_type
- label
- source_module
- section
- order
- visible
- status

5. Render text artifacts in preview.

For markdown:
- use markdown rendering if already available
- otherwise render content safely as simple text/HTML with paragraphs

Do not add a heavy editor dependency.

6. Include text artifacts in section display preview.

For now, use a simple internal renderer:

render_artifact <- function(artifact)

Behavior:
- plot artifact -> return artifact$object
- text artifact -> return a styled HTML block/card
- unsupported artifact -> return friendly placeholder

7. Do not change display_plots_grid() or display_plots_sections().

Those are plot-only.

For mixed artifacts, create app-side preview logic for now.

Do not add display_report_* to AutoPlots yet.

Output:
server <- function(input, output, session) {
  mapping_state <- reactiveValues(values = list())
  saved_plots <- reactiveValues(
    plots = list(),
    configs = list(),
    code = list(),
    metadata = list(),
    status = list()
  )
  saved_sections <- reactiveValues(
    sections = list()
  )
  plot_result <- reactiveVal(NULL)
  plot_error <- reactiveVal(NULL)
  plot_config <- reactiveVal(NULL)
  plot_list_message <- reactiveVal("")
  export_message <- reactiveVal("")
  project_message <- reactiveVal("")
  project_data <- reactiveVal(NULL)
  project_data_info <- reactiveVal(list(path = NULL, name = NULL))

  layout_cols_value <- function() {
    cols <- input$layout_cols
    if (is.null(cols) || is.na(cols)) {
      return(2)
    }

    as.integer(cols)
  }

  export_name_value <- function() {
    name <- selected_value(input$export_name)
    if (is.null(name)) {
      return(NULL)
    }

    tools::file_path_sans_ext(basename(name))
  }

  export_output_path <- function(export_dir, export_name) {
    file.path(export_dir, paste0(export_name, ".html"))
  }

  current_report_code <- function() {
    ready_names <- ready_plot_names()
    if (!length(ready_names)) {
      stop("No ready saved plots are available. Rebuild or repair saved plots before exporting R code.", call. = FALSE)
    }

    build_report_code(
      saved_code = ordered_list_by_names(saved_plots$code, ready_names),
      section_plot_names = section_plot_names(ready_only = TRUE),
      layout_type = input$layout_type,
      cols = layout_cols_value(),
      export_dir = default_value(selected_value(input$export_dir), "path/to/output"),
      export_name = default_value(export_name_value(), "autoplots_report"),
      data_path = default_value(current_data_path(), "path/to/data.csv")
    )
  }

  ordered_plot_names <- function() {
    ordered_plot_names_from_metadata(saved_plots$metadata)
  }

  ready_plot_names <- function() {
    plot_names <- ordered_plot_names()
    plot_names[vapply(plot_names, function(plot_name) {
      identical(saved_plots$status[[plot_name]]$status, "Ready")
    }, logical(1))]
  }

  ordered_saved_plots <- function() {
    ordered_list_by_names(saved_plots$plots, ready_plot_names())
  }

    saved_plots$metadata[[plot_name]] <- NULL

    for (section_name in names(sections)) {
      sections[[section_name]][[plot_name]] <- NULL
      if (!length(sections[[section_name]])) {
        sections[[section_name]] <- NULL
      }
    }
    saved_sections$sections <- sections

Output:
Implement the first internal table rendering, theme, and export framework.

Goal:
Create reusable table rendering, table theming, and table export helpers before adding table artifacts.

Rules:
- Do not add table artifacts yet.
- Do not modify AutoPlots.
- Do not use DT.
- Use reactable as the preferred rendering engine if available.
- Use simple htmltools table as fallback if reactable is unavailable.
- Use app-owned export helpers for CSV/XLSX.
- Keep R/ flat. Do not create subdirectories under R/.
- Use service_result for export helpers.
- Keep table styling consistent with the app UI themes: light, dark, and pimp.

Create or update:
R/table_render.R
R/table_builders.R
R/table_export.R
R/table_theme.R

Implement:

1. render_table()

render_table <- function(
  data,
  engine = c("reactable", "html"),
  title = NULL,
  subtitle = NULL,
  page_size = 10,
  searchable = TRUE,
  sortable = TRUE,
  filterable = FALSE,
  digits = 3,
  theme = c("auto", "light", "dark", "pimp")
)

Behavior:
- choose reactable when requested and available
- fallback to html table otherwise
- format numeric columns consistently
- render full table object for preview
- do not implement widget download buttons
- pass theme into the table theme helper
- if theme = "auto", use the current app theme if available, otherwise default to "light"

2. render_reactable_table()

Use reactable if installed/imported.

Behavior:
- apply a reactable theme from get_reactable_theme(theme)
- use consistent page size
- support searchable/sortable/filterable options
- format numeric columns using consistent digits
- keep styling compatible with app light/dark/pimp variables where practical
- do not overbuild

3. render_html_table()

Simple htmltools fallback.

Behavior:
- use app CSS classes
- respect light/dark/pimp theme through CSS variables where practical
- good enough for small tables

4. Add reactable theme helpers in R/table_theme.R

Implement:

get_reactable_theme <- function(theme = c("auto", "light", "dark", "pimp"))

It should return a reactable::reactableTheme object when reactable is available.

Also implement:

reactable_theme_light()
reactable_theme_dark()
reactable_theme_pimp()

5. Light reactable theme

Should feel consistent with light app theme:
- white table background
- light gray borders
- dark text
- subtle header background
- readable pagination/search controls

6. Dark reactable theme

Should feel consistent with dark ... [truncated]

Output:
Add table artifact support to the Analytics Shiny App.

Goal:
Allow users to create, preview, organize, display, and export table artifacts using the existing internal table framework.

Rules:
- Do not modify AutoPlots.
- Do not use DT.
- Do not create new table rendering logic outside the existing table framework.
- Use render_table() for table previews.
- Use export_table_csv() and export_table_xlsx() for table exports.
- Keep R/ flat. Do not create subdirectories under R/.
- Preserve existing plot and text artifact behavior.
- Keep changes small and targeted.

Requirements:

1. Add a Table Artifact creator UI.

Create a section/panel named:

Add Table Block

Inputs:
- table_artifact_label
- table_artifact_section
- table_artifact_type
- table_artifact_vars
- table_artifact_max_rows
- table_artifact_page_size
- table_artifact_theme

table_artifact_type choices:
- Data Preview
- Summary Statistics
- Frequency Table

table_artifact_theme choices:
- auto
- light
- dark
- pimp

Defaults:
- table_artifact_type = "Data Preview"
- table_artifact_max_rows = 25
- table_artifact_page_size = 10
- table_artifact_theme = "auto"

Actions:
- Preview Table
- Add Table Artifact

2. Build table data using existing table builder helpers.

Use:
- build_data_preview_table()
- build_summary_statistics_table()
- build_frequency_table()

Do not duplicate table builder logic in server observers.

3. Preview table artifacts.

Preview Table should:
- build the table data
- render it using render_table()
- show friendly validation errors if no data is uploaded or required variables are missing

4. Create table artifact on Add Table Artifact.

Use create_artifact():

create_artifact(
  artifact_id = next table artifact id, e.g. tbl1, tbl2, tbl3,
  artifact_type = "table",
  label = input$table_artifact_label,
  source_module = "table_builder",
  object = table_data,
  config = list(
    table_type = input$table_artifact_type,
    vars = input$table_artifact_vars,
    max_rows = input$table_artifact_max_rows,
    page_size = input$table_artifact_page_size,
    engine = "reactable",
    theme = input$table_artifact_theme
  ),
  code = generated table builder/render code if currently supported,
  metadata = list(
    n_rows = nrow(table_data),
    n_cols = ncol(table_data)
  ),
  section = input$table_artifact_section,
  order = next order,
  status = "ready"
)

5. Store table artifacts.

If the app already has generalized artifa ... [truncated]

server <- function(input, output, session) {
  mapping_state <- reactiveValues(values = list())
  saved_plots <- reactiveValues(
    plots = list(),
    configs = list(),
    code = list(),
    metadata = list(),
    status = list()
  )
  saved_sections <- reactiveValues(
    sections = list()
  )
  saved_text_artifacts <- reactiveValues(
    artifacts = list()
  )
  plot_result <- reactiveVal(NULL)
  plot_error <- reactiveVal(NULL)
  plot_config <- reactiveVal(NULL)
  plot_list_message <- reactiveVal("")
  text_artifact_message <- reactiveVal("")
  text_artifact_preview <- reactiveVal(NULL)
  export_message <- reactiveVal("")
  project_message <- reactiveVal("")
  project_data <- reactiveVal(NULL)
  project_data_info <- reactiveVal(list(path = NULL, name = NULL))

  layout_cols_value <- function() {
    cols <- input$layout_cols
    if (is.null(cols) || is.na(cols)) {
      return(2)
    }

    as.integer(cols)
  }

  export_name_value <- function() {
    name <- selected_value(input$export_name)
    if (is.null(name)) {
      return(NULL)
    }

    tools::file_path_sans_ext(basename(name))
  }

  export_output_path <- function(export_dir, export_name) {
    file.path(export_dir, paste0(export_name, ".html"))
  }

  current_report_code <- function() {
    ready_names <- ready_plot_names()
    if (!length(ready_names)) {
      stop("No ready saved plots are available. Rebuild or repair saved plots before exporting R code.", call. = FALSE)
    }

    build_report_code(
      saved_code = ordered_list_by_names(saved_plots$code, ready_names),
      section_plot_names = section_plot_names(ready_only = TRUE),
      layout_type = input$layout_type,

      export_name = default_value(export_name_value(), "autoplots_report"),
      data_path = default_value(current_data_path(), "path/to/data.csv")
    )
  }

  ordered_plot_names <- function() {
    ordered_plot_names_from_metadata(saved_plots$metadata)
  }

  ready_plot_names <- function() {
    plot_names <- ordered_plot_names()
    plot_names[vapply(plot_names, function(plot_name) {
      identical(saved_plots$status[[plot_name]]$status, "Ready")
    }, logical(1))]

    ordered_list_by_names(saved_plots$plots, ready_plot_names())

combined_artifact_summary <- function(plot_artifacts = list(), text_artifacts = list()) {
  artifacts <- c(plot_artifacts %||% list(), text_artifacts %||% list())
  summary <- artifact_summary(artifacts)
  if (!nrow(summary)) {
    return(summary)
  }

  summary[order(summary$order, summary$section, summary$artifact_id)]
}

render_artifact <- function(artifact) {
  if (!inherits(artifact, "aq_artifact")) {
    return(htmltools::tags$div(
      class = "aq-artifact-placeholder",
      "Unsupported artifact."
    ))
  }

  if (identical(artifact$artifact_type, "plot")) {
    return(artifact$object)
  }

  if (identical(artifact$artifact_type, "text")) {
    content <- artifact$content %||% ""
    paragraphs <- strsplit(content, "\\n\\s*\\n", perl = TRUE)[[1]]
    paragraphs <- paragraphs[nzchar(paragraphs)]
    if (!length(paragraphs)) {
      paragraphs <- "No text content."
    }

    return(htmltools::tags$article(
      class = "aq-text-artifact",
      htmltools::tags$h3(class = "aq-text-artifact-title", artifact$label),
      lapply(paragraphs, function(paragraph) {
        htmltools::tags$p(htmltools::HTML(htmltools::htmlEscape(paragraph)))
      })
    ))
  }

  htmltools::tags$div(
    class = "aq-artifact-placeholder",
    paste("Preview is not available for artifact type:", artifact$artifact_type)
  )
}

qa_artifact_model <- function() {

    code = "p1 <- AutoPlots::Line(...)",
    metadata = list(section_name = "Analysis", sort_order = 1L),
    section = "Analysis",
    order = 1L
  )

  text_artifact <- create_artifact(
    artifact_id = "n1",

## 308. 2026-07-04T18:42:27.219Z - analytics_shinyapp_continuation - user

Add an Artifact Library UI to the Analytics Shiny App.

Goal:
Create one central place to view, preview, organize, edit metadata, hide/show, export, and remove all report artifacts: plots, text blocks, and tables.

Rules:
- Do not modify AutoPlots.
- Do not use DT.
- Do not add GenAI yet.
- Do not add forecasting/modeling modules yet.
- Keep R/ flat. Do not create subdirectories under R/.
- Preserve existing plot, text, and table artifact behavior.
- Keep changes small and targeted.
- Use existing artifact_model.R helpers where possible.
- Use existing render_artifact() behavior where possible.
- Use existing table export helpers for table artifact export.

Requirements:

1. Add an Artifact Library page/tab/section.

The Artifact Library should show all artifacts:
- plot artifacts
- text artifacts
- table artifacts

Columns/cards should show:
- artifact_id
- artifact_type
- label
- source_module
- section
- order
- visible
- status

2. Use a single combined artifact summary.

Use or create:

combined_artifact_summary()

It should return one table/data.frame/data.table of all artifacts.

Do not maintain separate UI summaries for plots/text/tables if avoidable.

3. Add artifact selection.

Add:

selectInput("selected_artifact_id", "Artifact", choices = ...)

Update choices whenever artifacts change.

4. Add artifact preview.

When an artifact is selected, show a preview using:

render_artifact(selected_artifact)

Behavior:
- plot -> plot object
- text -> styled markdown/text block
- table -> render_table()
- unsupported -> friendly placeholder

5. Add metadata editing.

For selected artifact, allow editing:
- label
- section
- order
- visible

Actions:
- Update Artifact Metadata
- Hide / Show Artifact
- Remove Artifact

Do not edit artifact-specific configs yet unless already easy.
This task is only metadata/library management.

6. Add artifact type filters.

Add simple filters:
- All
- Plot
- Text
- Table

Optional if easy:
- filter by section
- filter by status

7. Add table export actions for table artifacts.

If selected artifact is a table, show:
- Export Table CSV
- Export Table XLSX

Use existing:
- export_table_csv()
- export_table_xlsx()

Export the full underlying table data, not the displayed reactable page.

8. Keep plot/text behavior unchanged.

Do not remove existing plot/text/table creation panels yet.
Artifact Library is a management layer, not a replacement yet.

9. Layout integration.

Layout/Display pages should continue using the selected ... [truncated]

server <- function(input, output, session) {
  mapping_state <- reactiveValues(values = list())
  saved_plots <- reactiveValues(
    plots = list(),
    configs = list(),
    code = list(),
    metadata = list(),
    status = list()
  )
  saved_sections <- reactiveValues(
    sections = list()
  )
  saved_text_artifacts <- reactiveValues(
    artifacts = list()
  )
  saved_table_artifacts <- reactiveValues(
    artifacts = list()
  )
  plot_result <- reactiveVal(NULL)
  plot_error <- reactiveVal(NULL)
  plot_config <- reactiveVal(NULL)
  plot_list_message <- reactiveVal("")
  text_artifact_message <- reactiveVal("")
  text_artifact_preview <- reactiveVal(NULL)
  table_artifact_message <- reactiveVal("")
  table_artifact_preview <- reactiveVal(NULL)
  export_message <- reactiveVal("")
  project_message <- reactiveVal("")
  project_data <- reactiveVal(NULL)
  project_data_info <- reactiveVal(list(path = NULL, name = NULL))

  layout_cols_value <- function() {
    cols <- input$layout_cols
    if (is.null(cols) || is.na(cols)) {
      return(2)
    }

    as.integer(cols)
  }

  export_name_value <- function() {
    name <- selected_value(input$export_name)
    if (is.null(name)) {
      return(NULL)
    }

    tools::file_path_sans_ext(basename(name))
  }

  export_output_path <- function(export_dir, export_name) {
    file.path(export_dir, paste0(export_name, ".html"))
  }

  current_report_code <- function() {
    ready_names <- ready_plot_names()
    if (!length(ready_names)) {
      stop("No ready saved plots are available. Rebuild or repair saved plots before exporting R code.", call. = FALSE)
    }

    build_report_code(
      saved_code = ordered_list_by_names(saved_plots$code, ready_names),
      section_plot_names = section_plot_names(ready_only = TRUE),
      layout_type = input$layout_type,
      cols = layout_cols_value(),
      export_dir = default_value(selected_value(input$export_dir), "path/to/output"),
      export_name = default_value(export_name_value(), "autoplots_report"),
      data_path = default_value(current_data_path(), "path/to/data.csv")
    )
  }

  ordered_plot_names <- function() {
    ordered_plot_names_from_metadata(saved_plots$metadata)
  }

  ready_plot_names <- function() {
    plot_names <- ordered_plot_names()
    plot_names[vapply(plot_names, function(plot_name) {
      identical(saved_plots$status[[plot_name]]$status, "Ready")
    }, logical(1))]
  }

  ordered_ ... [truncated]

  output$preview_plot <- renderUI({
    if (is.null(input$build_plot) || input$build_plot == 0L) {

        style = "padding: 16px; color: #6B7280;",
        "Configure the plot options, then click Build / Refresh Plot."
      ))
    }

    if (is.null(input$csv_file) && is.null(project_data())) {

    }

      )))
    }

    if (!is.null(plot_error())) {
      return(plot_error_message(plot_error()))

      ))

    text_artifact_message()

          plot_type = input$plot_type,

          mapping_values = mapping_state$values

        config$options
      } else {
        list()
      },
      section_names = names(section_plot_names()),
      selected_theme = input$theme
    )
  }

  rebuild_saved_plots <- function(data) {
    plots <- list()
    failures <- character()

    for (plot_name in names(saved_plots$configs)) {
      config <- saved_plots$configs[[plot_name]]
      compatibility <- plot_config_column_status(config, data = data)
      if (!identical(compatibility$status, "Ready")) {
        saved_plots$status[[plot_name]] <- compatibility
        failures <- c(failures, paste0(plot_name, ": ", compatibility$message))
        next
      }

      plot <- tryCatch(
        build_autoplots_call_from_config(config, data),
        error = function(e) {
          saved_plots$status[[plot_name]] <- list(
            status = "Rebuild failed",
            message = conditionMessage(e)
          )
          failures <<- c(failures, paste0(plot_name, ": ", conditionMessage(e)))
          NULL
        }
      )

      if (!is.null(plot)) {
        plots[[plot_name]] <- plot
        saved_plots$status[[plot_name]] <- list(
          status = "Ready",
          message = compatibility$message
        )
      }
    }

    saved_plots$plots <- plots
    failures
  }

  restore_project_ ... [truncated]

source(file.path("R", "utils_paths.R"), local = app_env)

dependency_check <- app_env$check_app_dependencies()

  stop(paste(dependency_check$messages, collapse = "\n"), call. = FALSE)
}

library(AutoPlots)

## 318. 2026-07-04T18:49:07.826Z - analytics_shinyapp_continuation - user

Modularize the Analytics Shiny App by product page.

Goal:
Move page-level UI/server logic out of the main app_ui.R and app_server.R files into flat R/ page files.

Rules:
- Do not add new product features.
- Do not change app behavior.
- Do not modify AutoPlots.
- Do not use DT.
- Keep R/ flat. Do not create subdirectories under R/.
- Do not over-modularize tiny UI components.
- Preserve all existing input/output behavior.
- Keep changes staged and reversible.

Create page files:

R/page_data.R
R/page_plot_builder.R
R/page_artifact_library.R
R/page_layouts.R
R/page_export.R
R/page_project.R

If settings already exists or theme settings are present, also create:

R/page_settings.R

Each page file should contain:
- page_<name>_ui <- function(id)
- page_<name>_server <- function(id, ...)

Use Shiny module conventions:
- NS(id) in UI
- moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) { ... }) in server

Shared state should be created once in app_server.R and passed into modules:
- data_state
- artifact_state
- project_state
- app_settings
- any existing message/status state

Responsibilities:

1. app_ui.R
Should become mostly shell/navigation/page placement.
It should call page_*_ui() functions.

2. app_server.R
Should become mostly shared state creation and module wiring.
It should call page_*_server() functions.

3. Data page
Owns:
- file upload
- data preview/status
- column info if present

4. Plot Builder page
Owns:
- plot type selection
- mapping controls
- plot options
- build/preview plot
- add/update plot artifact

5. Artifact Library page
Owns:
- combined artifact summary
- artifact filtering
- artifact selection
- artifact preview
- metadata editing
- hide/show/remove
- table artifact export actions

6. Layouts page
Owns:
- grid/section/mixed layout preview
- layout columns
- visible artifact ordering/section rendering

7. Export page
Owns:
- export directory/name
- HTML/R code/export all
- export status

8. Project page
Owns:
- save/load project
- save/load bundle
- project validation/status

9. Settings page, if present
Owns:
- app theme selection
- future GenAI settings placeholder if present

Constraints:
- Do not move core reusable logic into page files if it already belongs in service/helper files.
- Page modules should call existing service/helper functions.
- Avoid duplicating code during the move.
- Preserve generated code behavior.
- Preserve project save/load behavior.
- Preserve mixed artifact preview behavior.
- Preserve table renderi ... [truncated]

mapping_control <- function(mapping, data, required = TRUE, selected = NULL) {
  choices <- column_choices(data, include_none = !required)
  if (identical(mapping, "CorrVars")) {
    all_choices <- column_choices(data)
    if (is.null(selected)) {
      selected <- names(data)
    }

    selected <- selected[selected %in% all_choices]
    if (!length(selected)) {
      selected <- all_choices
    }

    return(selectInput(
      mapping_input_id(mapping),
      mapping,
      choices = all_choices,
      selected = selected,
      multiple = TRUE
    ))
  }

  if (is.null(selected) || !selected %in% choices) {
    if (!required && "" %in% choices) {
      selected <- ""
    } else {
      selected <- choices[1L]
    }
  }

  selectInput(
    mapping_input_id(mapping),
    mapping,
    choices = choices,
    selected = selected
  )
}
